speaker: Unknown
en: If we didn't have AI, we'd be in a panic right now about what's going to happen to the economy.
zh: 如果我们没有 AI，我们现在会对经济即将发生的事情感到恐慌。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've actually been in a regime for 50 years of very slow technological change in the face of declining population growth.
zh: 我们实际上处于一个 50 年的体制中，面对人口下降，技术变化非常缓慢。

speaker: Unknown
en: The timing has worked out miraculously well.
zh: 时机安排得奇迹般地好。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to have AI and robots precisely when we actually need them.
zh: 我们将拥有 AI 和机器人，恰恰是在我们真正需要它们的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: The remaining human workers are going to be at a premium, not at a discount.
zh: 剩余的人类工人将会变得溢价，而不是折价。

speaker: Unknown
en: How big of a deal is the moment in time that we are living through right now?
zh: 我们现在所处的这个时刻有多重要？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a very, very historic time.
zh: 这是一个非常、非常具有历史意义的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is the philosopher stone.
zh: AI 就是哲学之石。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, we have a technology that transfers the most common thing in the world, which is sand, converted into the most rare thing in the world which is thought.
zh: 现在，我们有了一种技术，它把世界上最常见的东西，即沙子，转化为世界上最稀缺的东西，即思想。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just spent a lot of time with the most cutting edge AI forward founders.
zh: 刚刚花了大量时间与最前沿的 AI 先行创始人在一起。

speaker: Unknown
en: The most leading edge founders are thinking of, can you have entire companies where the founder does everything?
zh: 最前沿的创始人在思考，你是否可以拥有创始人包办一切的公司？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's all this concern that young people, jobs are not going to be there for them, AI is replacing them.
zh: 人们普遍担心年轻人，工作将不再属于他们，AI 正在取代他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everybody wants to talk about job loss, but really, what you want to look at is task loss.
zh: 每个人都想谈论失业问题，但实际上，你想看的是任务损失。

speaker: Unknown
en: The job persists longer than the individual tasks.
zh: 职位存在的时间比单个任务更长。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's your sense of just the future of three very specific roles, product manager, engineer, designer?
zh: 对于三个非常具体的角色——产品经理、工程师、设计师——的未来，你有什么看法？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's like a Mexican standoff happening between those three roles.
zh: 这三个角色之间就像发生了一场墨西哥僵局。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every coder now believes they can also be a product manager and a designer because they have AI.
zh: 现在每个程序员都相信他们也可以成为产品经理和设计师，因为他们拥有 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every product manager thinks they can be a coder and a designer, and then every designer knows they can be a product manager and a coder.
zh: 每个产品经理都认为他们可以是程序员和设计师，然后每个设计师都知道他们可以是产品经理和程序员。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're actually all kind of correct.
zh: 他们实际上都正确到了某种程度。

speaker: Unknown
en: What happens is, the additive effect of being good at two things is more than double.
zh: 发生的情况是，擅长两件事的叠加效应不仅仅是翻倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: The additive effect of being good at three things is more than triple.
zh: 擅长三件事的叠加效应不仅仅是三倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: You become a super relevant specialist in the combination of the domains.
zh: 你成为了一个领域组合中超级相关的专家。

speaker: Unknown
en: People aren't fully grasping how much this is changing.
zh: 人们还没有完全掌握这在多大程度上正在改变事物。 [[23]】 真正想要提升自己并发展职业生涯的人，在我看来，此刻应该把每一个空闲小时都花在与 AI 交谈上，就像，“好吧，培训我一下。”

speaker: Unknown
en: People who really want to improve themselves and develop their career should be spending every spare hour, in my view, at this point, talking to AI, being like, "All right, train me up."
zh: 那些真正想要提升自己和发展职业生涯的人，在我看来，在这个阶段，应该把每一个空闲小时都用来和 AI 对话，就像，“好吧，训练我一下。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, my guest is Marc Andreessen, one of the most seminal figures in tech and in business.
zh: 今天，我的嘉宾是 Marc Andreessen，科技和商业界最具开创性的人物之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: He invented the web browser, built the world's largest venture firm.
zh: 他发明了网络浏览器，建立了世界上最大的风险投资公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's also a multi-time founder and an investor in essentially every generational tech company, and is also one of the most clear-minded, lateral, and insightful thinkers about both the past and the future of technology.
zh: 他也是一位多次创业的创始人，并且投资了几乎每一代科技公司，也是对过去和未来技术最清醒、横向和深刻的思考者之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: In this very special conversation, we chat about how unique and significant the moment that we are all living through right now is, what skills he's teaching his kids to thrive in the AI future, what happens to product managers, designers, and engineers in the coming years, where moats exist in AI, what the most AI native founders are doing differently, and so much more that is just scratching the surface of this very deep and important conversation.
zh: 在这次非常特别的对话中，我们聊到了我们所有人现在所处的时刻是多么独特和重要，他正在教给他的孩子们什么技能以便在 AI 未来中茁壮成长，产品经理、设计师和工程师在未来几年会发生什么，AI 中的护城河存在于哪里，最 AI 原生的创始人做有什么不同，以及更多内容，这只是触及了这次非常深层且重要对话的表面。

speaker: Unknown
en: You are going to walk away from this chat being smarter about what is going on in the world right now and where things are heading.
zh: 听完这次对话后，你会对现在世界上正在发生的事情以及未来的走向变得更加聪明。

speaker: Unknown
en: A huge thank you to my newsletter community and folks on X for suggesting topics and questions for this conversation.
zh: 非常感谢我的通讯社区和 X 上的朋友们为这次对话建议了主题和问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you enjoy this podcast, don't forget to subscribe and follow it in your favorite podcasting app or YouTube.
zh: 如果你喜欢这个播客，不要忘记在你最喜欢的播客应用程序或 YouTube 上订阅并关注它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It helps tremendously.
zh: 这会有巨大的帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you become an insider subscriber of my newsletter, you get a year free of over 20 incredible products, including a year free of Lovable, Replit, Bolt, Gamma, Innate and Linear, Superhuman, Dev and PostHog, Descript, Wspr Flow, Perplexity, Warp, Granola, Magic Patterns, Raycast, Chappy, RD, MOB and Stripe Atlas.
zh: 如果你成为我通讯的内部订阅者，你可以免费一年使用 20 多种令人难以置信的产品，包括免费一年 Lovable、Replit、Bolt、Gamma、Innate 和 Linear、Superhuman、Dev 和 PostHog、Descript、Wspr Flow、Perplexity、Warp、Granola、Magic Patterns、Raycast、Chappy、RD、MOB 和 Stripe Atlas。

speaker: Unknown
en: Head on over to lennysnewsletter.com and click Product Pass.
zh: 请前往 lennysnewsletter.com 并点击 Product Pass。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, I bring you Marc Andreessen after a short word from our sponsors.
zh: 话虽如此，在简短的赞助商致辞之后，我为您带来 Marc Andreessen。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today's episode is brought to you by DX, the developer intelligence platform designed by leading researchers.
zh: 今天的节目由 DX 带给您，DX 是由领先研究人员设计的开发者智能平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: To thrive in the AI era, organizations need to adapt quickly, but many organization leaders struggle to answer pressing questions like, which tools are working?
zh: 为了在 AI 时代茁壮成长，组织需要快速适应，但许多组织领导者在回答紧迫问题时遇到困难，比如，哪些工具有效？

speaker: Unknown
en: How are they being used?
zh: 它们是如何被使用的？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's actually driving value?
zh: 究竟是什么在驱动价值？

speaker: Unknown
en: DX provides the data and insights that leaders need to navigate this shift.
zh: DX 提供领导者驾驭这一转变所需的数据和洞察。

speaker: Unknown
en: With DX, companies like Dropbox, Booking.com, Adient, and Intercom get a deep understanding of how AI is providing value to their developers and what impact AI is having on engineering productivity.
zh: 有了 DX，像 Dropbox、Booking.com、Adient 和 Intercom 这样的公司可以深入了解 AI 如何为他们的开发者提供价值，以及 AI 对工程生产力有什么影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: To learn more, visit DX's website at getdx.com/lenny.
zh: 要了解更多，请访问 DX 网站 getdx.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's getdx.com/lenny.
zh: 网址是 getdx.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're a founder, the hardest part of starting a company isn't having the idea, it's scaling the business without getting buried in back office work.
zh: 如果你是一位创始人，创业中最难的部分不是拥有想法，而是在不被后台工作埋没的情况下扩展业务。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's where Brex comes in.
zh: 这就是 Brex 发挥作用的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Brex is the intelligent finance platform for founders.
zh: Brex 是面向创始人的智能金融平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: With Brex, you get high limit corporate cards, easy banking, high yield treasury, plus a team of AI agents that handle manual finance tasks for you.
zh: 有了 Brex，你就可以获得高额度企业卡、轻松的银行业务、高收益国库券，外加一组 AI 智能体为你处理手动金融任务。

speaker: Unknown
en: They'll do all the stuff that you don't want to do, like file your expenses, scour transactions for waste, and run reports all according to your rules.
zh: 它们会做你不想做的一切事情，比如报销你的开支， scour 交易记录以寻找浪费，并根据你的规则运行报告。

speaker: Unknown
en: With Brex's AI agents, you can move faster while staying in full control.
zh: 有了 Brex 的 AI 智能体，你可以在保持完全控制的同时行动得更快。

speaker: Unknown
en: One in three startups in the United States already runs on Brex.
zh: 在美国，三分之一的初创公司已经在 Brex 上运营。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can too at brex.com.
zh: 你也可以在 brex.com 上做到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Marc Andreessen, thank you so much for being here and welcome to the podcast.
zh: Marc Andreessen，非常感谢你来到这里并欢迎参加播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome, Lenny.
zh: 太棒了，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you.
zh: 谢谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's great to be here.
zh: 很高兴来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to start with just a big picture question.
zh: 我想先从一个大局观的问题开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have a billion directions I want to go, but I think this is going to give us a little bit of a frame of reference.
zh: 我有一百个方向想聊，但我认为这会给我们一点参考框架。

speaker: Unknown
en: How big of a deal is the moment in time that we are living through right now?
zh: 我们现在所处的这个时刻究竟有多大的影响？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a very, very historic time.
zh: 这是一个非常、非常具有历史意义的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think 2025 was maybe the most interesting year in my entire career and probably life, and I think I would expect 2026 to exceed that.
zh: 我认为 2025 年可能是我整个职业生涯中甚至是我一生中最有趣的一年，而且我认为我会预期 2026 年会超过它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wow, that says a lot.
zh: 哇，这很有说明力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I've seen some stuff.
zh: 是的，我见识过一些事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it feels like two things are happening.
zh: 所以感觉有两件事正在发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is, the trust that a lot of people have had and kind of what you described as kind of legacy institutions around the world is, I think, in kind of full-scale collapse right now.
zh: 一件是，很多人曾经拥有的、以及你所描述的那种世界各地的传统机构中的信任，我认为目前正处于某种全面崩溃之中。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, there's a lot of data to support that.
zh: 顺便说一下，有很多数据支持这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think there's a lot of structures, orders, and institutions that people have just relied on for a long time that have just proven to not be up for the challenge.
zh: 所以我认为有很多人们长期以来依赖的结构、秩序和机构，已经被证明无法应对挑战。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then kind of corresponding with that is, the national and global conversation have become, let's say, liberated.
zh: 然后与此相对应的是，国家和全球的对话已经变得，比如说，解放了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so this sort of incredible revolution that we have in what I've described as freedom of speech, freedom of thought, ability for people to openly discuss things that maybe they couldn't discuss even a few years ago has just dramatically expanded.
zh: 因此，这种我所描述的言论自由、思想自由、人们能够公开讨论那些也许几年前甚至无法讨论的事情的不可思议的革命，已经急剧扩大了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's now on a one way train for just a much broader range of discourse.
zh: 我认为这现在已经是一列单向列车，通向更广泛的言论范围。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there's also just these incredibly massive geopolitical shifts that are happening.
zh: 然后还有正在发生的极其巨大的地缘政治转变。

speaker: Unknown
en: And obviously, the US is changing a lot, Europe is changing a lot, China's changing a lot.
zh: 显然，美国正在发生很大变化，欧洲正在发生很大变化，中国正在发生很大变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Latin America, by the way, is changing a lot.
zh: 拉丁美洲，顺便说一下，也正在发生很大变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Very dramatic events playing out down there right now.
zh: 目前那里正在上演非常戏剧性的事件。

speaker: Unknown
en: Kind of all over the world, I think a lot of assumptions are being pulled out into the daylight and reexamined.
zh: 我在世界各地都认为，很多假设正被曝光到日光下并重新审查。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it's kind of the fact that all these things are happening at the same time.
zh: 然后事实是，所有这些事情都在同时发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you've got all of these countries and industries where things are kind of increasingly upheaval, but you have AI as this kind of new technology that's going to really affect things.
zh: 所以你拥有所有这些国家和行业，那里的情况越来越动荡，但你拥有 AI 这种真正会影响到事情的新技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you've got people, citizens being able to fully participate and being able to argue things out.
zh: 然后你还有人们、公民能够充分参与并能够把事情辩论清楚。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's kind of like those three big mega things are all colliding at the same time.
zh: 所以就像这三件大事都在同一时间发生碰撞。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we're probably just at the very beginning of all three of those.
zh: 而且我认为我们可能才刚刚处于这三件事的开端。

speaker: Unknown
en: And those all feel like historical moment shifts, comparable in magnitude to maybe default the Berlin Wall in 1989, maybe the end of World War II, kind of moments like that.
zh: 这些感觉像是历史性的时刻转变，其规模大概可以比作 1989 年柏林墙的倒塌，也许是二战的结束，诸如此类的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: It certainly feels like that.
zh: 感觉确实像那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Good God.
zh: 天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: What a time to be alive.
zh: 活在这个时代真了不起。

speaker: Marc Andreessen
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: In terms of the AI piece, which is where a lot of people are trying to figure out what to do, what do you think isn't being priced in yet in terms of the impact AI is going to have on, say, the world or just people listening?
zh: 在 AI 方面，这也是很多人试图弄清楚该怎么做的地方，你认为在 AI 将对世界或仅仅是听众产生的影响方面，还有什么没有被计入价格？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think, at this point, it's pretty clear, with our technology hats on, that this stuff is really working now.
zh: 我认为，在这一点上，戴上我们的技术帽子来看，很明显这东西现在真的有效了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There was a ChatGPT moment three years ago.
zh: 三年前有过一个 ChatGPT 时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, only three years ago, was the ChatGPT moment.
zh: 顺便说一下，就在三年前，那是 ChatGPT 时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the big question was, all right, this is incredibly fun and creative.
zh: 当时的大问题是，好吧，这非常有趣和有创意。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we have machines now that can compose Shakespearean sonnets and rap lyrics, and this is amazing.
zh: 我们现在有可以创作莎士比亚十四行诗和说唱歌词的机器，这太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then there was this big question, can you harness this technology for reasoning and for problem solving in domains that really matter, medicine, science, law and so forth?
zh: 但当时有一个大问题，你能在真正重要的领域，如医学、科学、法律等，利用这项技术进行推理和解决问题吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it turns out the answer to that is yes.
zh: 结果证明答案是肯定的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the last 12 months, and especially even just the last three months have really proven that AI can really do... I mean, you're seeing it all now.
zh: 过去的 12 个月，甚至特别是过去的 3 个月，真的证明了 AI 真的可以……我是说，你现在全都看到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is now developing new math theorems.
zh: AI 现在正在开发新的数学定理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Over the holiday break, it feels like the AI coding thing really hit critical mass and the world's best programmers, including Linus Torvalds, for the first time over the holiday break basically said, "Yeah, AI is now coding better than we can."
zh: 在假期休息期间，感觉 AI 编码这件事真的达到了临界质量，世界上最好的程序员，包括 Linus Torvalds，在假期休息期间第一次基本上说，“是的，AI 现在编代码比我们好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's incredibly powerful.
zh: 所以这非常强大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we all assume that AI now is going to get really good at reasoning in any domain in which there are verifiable answers.
zh: 我认为我们都假设 AI 现在将在任何有可验证答案的领域中变得非常擅长推理。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's going to include many very important domains.
zh: 所以这将包括许多非常重要的领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the technology feels like it's moving fast and it's going to be working really well.
zh: 所以技术感觉发展很快，而且会运作得非常好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the thing that is not well understood, I think a lot of people in the industry have kind of what I would describe as this one dimensional thing, which is, okay, as a result of the technology now working, AI just kind of sweeps the world and changes everything.
zh: 我认为有一件事没有被很好地理解，我认为行业中很多人有一种我所描述的一维观点，即，好吧，由于技术现在起作用了，AI 只是横扫世界并改变一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's kind of the wrong framer.
zh: 我认为这是一种错误的框架。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's based on an incomplete understanding of the world that we live in or the world that we've been living in for the last 80 years.
zh: 我认为这是基于对我们生活的世界或我们过去 80 年生活的世界的不完整理解。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I would call out two things in particular.
zh: 我想特别指出两件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: So one is, I think it's felt to us, in the US and the West, for the last whatever, 30 years or 50 years, it's felt like we've been in a time of great technological change.
zh: 首先，对于我们，在美国和西方，在过去 30 年或 50 年里，感觉就像我们处在一个巨大的技术变革时期。

speaker: Unknown
en: But actually, if you look for actually evidence of that, in statistical evidence of that, analytical evidence of that, you basically can't find it.
zh: 但实际上，如果你寻找实际证据，在统计数据证据，分析证据中，你基本上找不到。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in particular, economists have a way of measuring the rate of technological change in the economy that is productivity growth, which we could talk about what that means, but basically, it's sort of the mathematical expression of the impact of technology on the economy.
zh: 特别是，经济学家有一种方法来衡量经济中技术变革的速率，那就是生产率增长，我们可以讨论这意味着什么，但基本上，它是技术对经济影响的数学表达。

speaker: Unknown
en: And productivity growth for the last 50 years has actually been very low, not very high.
zh: 而过去 50 年的生产率增长实际上非常低，而不是很高。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we all feel like it's been very high.
zh: 所以我们都感觉它非常高。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's been lots of technological change.
zh: 有很多技术变革。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's actually happening is, it's been very low.
zh: 实际发生的情况是，它非常低。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, the pace of productivity growth, like in the US, is running at a half of what it... In my lifetime, in our lifetimes, it's been running at about half the pace that it ran between 1940 and 1970, and it's been running at about a third the pace that it ran between about 1870 to about 1940.
zh: 事实上，生产率增长的速度，比如在美国，运行在我有生之年，在我们的有生之年，它的速度大约是 1940 年到 1970 年间速度的一半，大约是 1870 年到 1940 年间速度的三分之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so statistically, in the US, in the West, technology progress in the economy, technology impact in the economy has actually slowed way down.

zh: 教育领域有一个巨大的问题，即，如何提高教育成果？

speaker: Unknown
en: And basically, it turns out it's very hard to improve educational outcomes except there's one method that always does it, which is called the Bloom's 2 Sigma effect, which is there's one method of education that routinely raises student outcomes by two standards of deviation and will take a kid from the 50th percentile to the 99th percentile and that's one-on-one tutoring.
zh: 基本上，结果证明很难提高教育成果，除了有一种方法总能做到，这叫做布鲁姆的 2 西格玛效应，即有一种教育方法能例行公事般地将学生成果提高两个标准差，将孩子从第 50 百分位数提升到第 99 百分位数，这就是一对一辅导。

speaker: Unknown
en: So again, if you go back to N=1, you have a kid and a tutor and they're in this very tight loop with each other, where the kid is able to constantly kind of be on the leading edge of what they're capable of doing and they can move incredibly fast and they get kind of correction in real time, you get these better outcomes.
zh: 所以再说一次，如果你回到 N=1，你有一个孩子和一个导师，他们彼此处于一个非常紧密的循环中，孩子能够不断地处于他们能力所能及的最前沿，他们可以移动得非常快，并且能实时得到纠正，你会得到更好的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: But to your question, it's never been economically feasible for anybody other than the richest people in society to be able to provide one-on-one tutoring for kids.
zh: 但对于你的问题，除了社会上最富有的人之外，为任何其他人提供一对一辅导在经济上从未可行过。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI provides the very real prospect of being able to do that, because obviously now, if you have a kid that's super interested in something and they can talk to an LLM about it and they can ask an infinite number of questions and they can get instantaneous feedback.
zh: AI 提供了非常真实的前景来做到这一点，因为显然现在，如果你有一个孩子对某事非常感兴趣，他们可以和 LLM 讨论这件事，他们可以问无限数量的问题，并且能获得即时反馈。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, you can even tell an LLM, it's like, "Teach me how to do the following."
zh: 事实上，你甚至可以告诉 LLM，就像，“教我如何做以下事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can say, "Wow, I don't quite understand what you're saying.
zh: 你可以说，“哇，我不太明白你在说什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Numb it down for me a little bit."
zh: 对我来说稍微弱化一点。”

speaker: Unknown
en: "Okay, now quiz me, do I actually understand this?"
zh: “好的，现在考考我，我真的明白了吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: People can just do this today.
zh: 人们今天就可以做这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think there's this massive opportunity for parents in many walks of life, with a little bit of time at focus, to be able to say, " Okay, my kid's probably still going to go through a traditional education system, but I'm going to augment this with AI tutoring."
zh: 所以我认为各行各业、只要有一点时间投入的父母，都有这个巨大的机会，能够说，“好的，我的孩子可能还是会经历传统的教育体系，但我打算用 AI 辅导来增强它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course, there's going to be tons of startups, and there already are, that are going to try to build on all the products and services for this.
zh: 当然，会有大量的初创公司，实际上已经有了，将尝试为此构建所有的产品和服务。

speaker: Unknown
en: Khan Academy, on the nonprofit side, has a big push to do this.
zh: Khan Academy，在非营利方面，大力推动这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think the broad answer might be a hybrid approach with schools plus one-to-one tutoring through AI.
zh: 所以我认为广泛的答案可能是一种混合方法，结合学校加上通过 AI 进行一对一辅导。

speaker: Unknown
en: You may have heard, there's this great new private school system called Alpha, in which everything I just described is kind of the basis of their philosophy, which is, it's a combination of in person schools and teachers, but it's also heavily based on AI and AI tutoring.
zh: 你可能听说过，有一个很棒的新的私立学校体系叫 Alpha，我刚才描述的所有事情都是他们哲学的基础，也就是，它是面对面学校和老师的结合，但同时也严重依赖 AI 和 AI 辅导。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think there is a magic formula in here that I think is going to apply much more broadly.
zh: 所以我认为这里有一个神奇的公式，我认为它会广泛得多的适用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And really, for parents interested in this, now it'd be a great time to really start to think hard about that and to look at the options.
zh: 对于对此感兴趣的父母，现在真的开始认真思考这个问题并审视这些选择的好时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's interesting because there's all this concern that young people, jobs are not going to be there for them, AI is replacing them.
zh: 这很有趣，因为有这么多的担忧，年轻人，工作不会在那里了，AI 会取代他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: On the flip side, there's what you're describing here.
zh: 另一方面，有你在这里描述的这种情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels like people coming in learning today are going to move so fast and learn so much more.
zh: 感觉现在进入学习的人会移动得非常快，学得更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And where do you sit on this divide of, young people are in big trouble or they're actually going to be the ones winning in the end?
zh: 在这个分歧中你站在哪一边，年轻人有大麻烦还是他们最终会成为赢家？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the job substitution, job loss thing is just, it's very reductive.
zh: 所以工作替代、工作流失这件事，只是，非常简化。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's an overly simplistic model.
zh: 我认为这是一个过度简化的模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, it goes back to what I said at the very beginning, which is, we've actually been in a regime for 50 years of very slow technological change in the economy.
zh: 而且，这又回到了我在一开始所说的，实际上我们已经在经济中技术变化非常缓慢的体制中 50 年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so again, like I said, it's at half the rate of the previous era and then a third of the rate of 100 years ago.
zh: 而且，像我说的，它是以前时代速率的一半，然后是 100 年前速率的三分之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we're coming out of this kind of phase where we've had almost no technological progress in the economy, we've had remarkably little job churn as a result of that relative to any historical period.
zh: 所以我们正在走出这个阶段，在这个阶段我们在经济中几乎没有技术进步，相对于任何历史时期，结果我们的工作变动非常少。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so even if AI ticks up, even if AI triples productivity growth in the economy, which would be a massively big deal, it would take us back to the same level of job churn that was happening between 1870 and 1930.
zh: 所以即使 AI 上升，即使 AI 使经济中的生产率增长翻三倍，这将是一件大事，这也会让我们回到 1870 年到 1930 年之间发生的同样的工作变动水平。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you go back and you read accounts of 1870 to 1930, people just thought the world was a watch with opportunity.
zh: 如果你回去并阅读 1870 年到 1930 年的记录，人们只是认为世界是一个充满了机会的怀表。

speaker: Unknown
en: At that rate of technological transformation, kids were able to develop new careers into new areas of the economy, building new kinds of products and services.
zh: 在这种技术变革率下，孩子们能够发展到经济的新领域的新职业，构建新的产品和服务。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, a huge part of everything in our modern world today was kind of invented and proliferated during that period.
zh: 我是说，今天现代世界中的一切，很大一部分都是在那个时期发明和普及的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so even if AI triples the pace of economic change in the economy, it's going to just translate to a much higher rate of economic growth, it's going to translate to a much higher rate of job growth.
zh: 所以即使 AI 使经济中经济变化的步伐翻三倍，这也将转化为更高的经济增长率，转化为更高的就业增长率。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there'll be some level of task level and job level substitution that will take place, but that will be swamped by the macro effects of economic growth and innovation that will happen.
zh: 将会有某种程度的任务级和作业级替代发生，但这将被经济增长和创新的宏观效应所淹没。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then corresponding to that, there'll be hiring booms, quite honestly, I think all over the place.
zh: 相应地，将会有雇佣热潮，坦率地说，我认为到处都会发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then again, go back to the other thing, which is like, this is all happening in the face of declining population growth and increasingly population shrinkage.
zh: 然后再回到另一件事，就是，所有这一切都是在人口增长下降和人口日益萎缩的情况下发生的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so human workers in many, many, many countries over the next 10, 20, 30 years are going to be at more and more of a premium, literally because you're going to have shrinking population levels.
zh: 因此，在未来 10 年、20 年、30 年里，许多许多许多国家的人类工人将变得越来越稀缺，实际上是因为你将面临人口水平的下降。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't really want to get into politics particularly, but it does feel like the world broadly is going to reverse course on the rates of immigration that we've had for the last 50 years.
zh: 我们并不想特别涉足政治，但感觉全世界似乎正准备扭转过去 50 年来的移民率趋势。

speaker: Unknown
en: It seems to be kind of a broad-based thing happening with rise to nationalism, concerns about the rate of immigration and immigration historically in countries like the US, it's kind of ebbed and flowed over time based on how the national mood shifts.
zh: 看这似乎是一种普遍发生的事情，随着民族主义的兴起，对移民率以及历史上移民的担忧，在美国这样的国家，它随着国家情绪的变化而随时间起伏。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if you sort of combine in a country like the US or any country in Europe, if you combine declining population with less immigration, the remaining human workers are going to be at a premium, not at a discount.
zh: 因此，如果你把像美国这样的国家或欧洲的任何国家结合起来，如果你把人口减少与更少的移民结合起来，剩下的人类工人将变得稀缺，而不是过剩。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that combination of faster productivity growth, faster economic growth, and then slower population growth and less immigration actually means there's going to be much less of this kind of dystopian no jobs' thing.
zh: 因此，我认为生产率更快增长、经济增长更快，然后人口增长更慢和移民更少这种组合，实际上意味着这种反乌托邦的「没有工作」的事情会少得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just think it's probably totally off pace.
zh: 我只是认为这可能完全偏离了节奏。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is extremely interesting.
zh: 这非常有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I'm hearing is, you're not super worried about job loss.
zh: 所以我听到的是，你并不太担心失业。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is the key here that the timing kind of just works out, this population decrease, all these kind of have to line up for there not to be this massive job loss with AI?
zh: 这里的关键在于时机是否恰好合适，这种人口减少，所有这些都必须协调一致，才不会出现 AI 带来的这种大规模失业吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, look, if we didn't have AI, we'd be in a panic right now about what's going to happen to the economy.
zh: 嗯，听着，如果我们没有 AI，我们现在就会对经济将发生什么感到恐慌。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because what we'd be staring at is a future of depopulation.
zh: 因为我们将面临的是一个人口减少的未来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And depopulation without new technology would just mean that the economy shrinks.
zh: 如果没有新技术，人口减少将意味着经济萎缩。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it would mean that the economy kind of itself kind of shrinks over time.
zh: 所以这意味着经济本身会随时间推移而萎缩。

speaker: Unknown
en: The opportunity diminishes.
zh: 机会减少了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are no new jobs, there are no new fields, there's no new source of consumer demand for spending on things.
zh: 没有新工作，没有新领域，没有新的消费需求来源来花钱买东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you would be very worried about going into a period of severe decline of stagnation.
zh: 所以你会非常担心进入一个严重衰退或停滞的时期。

speaker: Unknown
en: And essentially, you'd be looking at these very dystopian scenarios of an economy kind of self-euthanizing itself over time.
zh: 本质上，你会看到这些非常反乌托邦的情景，经济随时间推移自我安乐死。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you'd be very worried about the opposite of what everybody thinks that they're worried about.
zh: 所以你会非常担心每个人都认为他们正在担心的相反情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: The only reason we're not worried about that is because we now know that we have the technology that can substitute for the lack of population growth and then also for the lack of immigration that's likely.
zh: 我们唯一不担心那一点的原因是因为我们现在知道我们拥有可以替代人口增长不足的技术，并且还可以替代可能的移民不足。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I would say the timing has worked out miraculously well in the sense of, we're going to have AI and robots precisely when we actually need them, to keep the economy from actually shrinking.
zh: 所以我想说时机奇迹般地奏效了，在这个意义上，我们将在我们真正需要 AI 和机器人的时候拥有它们，以防止经济真正萎缩。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just think that's just a fundamentally good news story.
zh: 我只是认为这根本就是一个好消息的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: To get to the mass job loss thing that people are worried about on the other side of things, you'd have to look at far, far, far higher rates of productivity growth.
zh: 要达到人们担心的另一面的大规模失业，你需要看到的生产率增长速度要高得多，高得多，高得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: You'd have to look at rates of productivity growth that are 10, 20, 30, 50% a year, something like that, which are orders of magnitude higher than we've ever had in an economy in the history of the planet.
zh: 你需要看到的生产率增长速度是每年 10%、20%、30%、50%，诸如此类，这比我们在地球历史上经济中有过的速度高出几个数量级。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's possible that we get that.
zh: 我们有可能会得到那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, look, I have my utopian temptation along with everybody else.
zh: 我的意思是，听着，我和其他人一样都有我的乌托邦式诱惑。

speaker: Unknown
en: If AI radically transforms everything overnight, then maybe let's play out the kind of utopian-
zh: 如果 AI 一夜之间彻底改变了所有事情，那么也许让我们推演一下这种乌托邦式的——

speaker: Unknown
en: ... radically transforms everything overnight.
zh: ……彻底改变所有事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then maybe let's play out the kind of utopian scenario.
zh: 那么也许让我们推演一下这种乌托邦式的情景。

speaker: Unknown
en: You get to a much higher level of productivity growth, you get to much higher level of technological change.
zh: 你将达到更高的生产率增长水平，你将达到更高的技术变革水平。

speaker: Unknown
en: Corresponding to that, you'll have a massive economic boom.
zh: 与此相对应，你将拥有巨大的经济繁荣。

speaker: Unknown
en: You'll have a massive growth in the economy.
zh: 你将拥有巨大的经济增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then corresponding with that, you'll have a collapse in prices.
zh: 然后与此相对应，你将面临价格崩盘。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the price of goods and services that are, whatever you're going to call it, affected by or commoditized by AI, the prices of those goods and services will collapse.
zh: 因此，受 AI 影响或被 AI 商品化的商品和服务的价格，这些商品和服务的价格将崩盘。

speaker: Unknown
en: There'll be price deflation.
zh: 会出现通货紧缩。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then as a consequence of price deflation, everything that people are buying today gets a lot cheaper.
zh: 然后，作为通货紧缩的结果，人们今天购买的所有东西都变得更便宜了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's the equivalent of a gigantic increase in wealth across the society, right?
zh: 这相当于整个社会的财富巨大增加，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Take it this way.
zh: 这样理解吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is actually worth talking about because people, I think, get sideways on this issue.
zh: 这实际上值得讨论一下，因为我认为人们在这个问题上会陷入困惑。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if AI is going to transform the economy as much as the, whatever, utopians or dystopians or whatever kind of thing that it will, the necessary economic calculation of what happens is massive productivity growth.
zh: 所以如果 AI 将像乌托邦主义者或反乌托邦主义者或任何那样改变经济，那么对发生的事情的必要经济计算就是大规模的生产率增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: The consequence of massive productivity growth, what that literally means mechanically is more output requiring less input.
zh: 大规模生产率增长的结果，它在字面上和机械上意味着更少的投入产生更多的产出。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you get more economic output for less input.
zh: 所以，你用更少的投入获得更多的经济产出。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you're substituting in AI for human workers or whatever.
zh: 所以你用 AI 代替人类工人或任何东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as a consequence, you get this massive boom in output with much lower input costs.
zh: 结果，你在投入成本低得多的情况下获得了产出的巨大繁荣。

speaker: Unknown
en: The result of that is you get gluts of goods and services in all those affected sectors.
zh: 其结果是，在所有受影响的部门都会出现商品和服务的过剩。

speaker: Unknown
en: The result of those gluts is you get collapsing prices.
zh: 这些过剩的结果是你面临价格崩盘。

speaker: Unknown
en: The collapsing prices mean that the thing today that costs you $100, now costs you $10, and now costs you $1.
zh: 价格崩盘意味着今天你花 100 美元的东西，现在要花你 10 美元，现在要花你 1 美元。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the equivalent of giving everybody a giant raise because now they have all this additional spending power.
zh: 这相当于给每个人加薪，因为现在他们拥有所有这些额外的购买力。

speaker: Unknown
en: That additional spending power then translates to economic growth, the development of new fields.
zh: 那种额外的购买力随后转化为经济增长，新领域的发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everybody's materially much better off very quickly.
zh: 每个人的物质条件都非常快地变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then by the way, to the extent that you do have unemployment coming out the other side of that, it's now much cheaper to provide the social safety net to prevent people from being immiserated because the prices of all the goods and services that a welfare program has to pay from, they're all collapsing.
zh: 顺便说一下，在某种程度上，如果在那之后确实出现了失业，那么现在提供社会安全网以防止人们陷入贫困要便宜得多，因为福利计划必须支付的所有商品和服务的价格，它们都在暴跌。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, the price of healthcare collapses, the price of housing collapses, the price of education collapses, the price of everything else collapses because of this incredible impact that AI is having.
zh: 所以，医疗保健的价格崩盘，住房的价格崩盘，教育的价格崩盘，其他所有东西的价格都崩盘，因为 AI 正产生这种不可思议的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so in this kind of utopian/dystopian scenario that people have, there's no scenario in which everybody's just poor.
zh: 所以，在人们设想的这种乌托邦/反乌托邦的场景中，不存在所有人都变得贫穷的场景。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, it's quite the opposite, which is everybody gets a lot richer because prices collapse.
zh: 事实上，情况恰恰相反，那就是每个人都变得更富裕了，因为价格崩盘了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it's actually much easier to pay for the social safety net for the people who, for some reason, can't find a job.
zh: 然后，对于那些由于某种原因找不到工作的人来说，为他们支付社会安全网实际上要容易得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so maybe we end up in that scenario.
zh: 所以也许我们最终会处于那种场景中。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, the optimistic part of me says, "Yeah, maybe AI is that powerful, and maybe the rest of the economy can actually change to accommodate that, and maybe that'll happen."
zh: 我是说，我乐观的一面说，“是的，也许 AI 就是那么强大，也许经济的其他部分实际上可以改变以适应这种强大，也许这会发生。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But the result of that is going to be a much better news story than people think it's going to be.
zh: 但那个结果将会是一个比人们认为的要好得多的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, everything I've just described, by the way, is just a very straightforward extrapolation of very basic economics.
zh: 另外，顺便说一句，我刚才描述的一切，都只是对非常基础的经济学的非常直接的推断。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not making any bold predictions of what I just said.
zh: 对于我刚才说的话，我没有做出任何大胆的预测。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is just a straightforward mechanical process that plays itself out if you have higher rates of productivity growth, which are necessarily the results of higher rates of technological growth.
zh: 这只是一个直接的机械过程，如果你有更高的生产率增长率，它就会自动演绎出来，而这必然是更高技术增长率的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I think we're looking at... And to be clear, I think we're looking at a world that's not radically transformed the way that, maybe, the utopians think that it will be or the dystopians think it will be.
zh: 所以，我认为我们正在关注……明确地说，我认为我们正在关注一个世界，它并没有像乌托邦主义者认为的那样发生彻底的转变，或者像反乌托邦主义者认为的那样发生彻底的转变。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it'll be more incremental for reasons we can discuss, but I think that incremental, overwhelmingly, I think that process is going to be a good news process.
zh: 出于我们可以讨论的原因，我认为它更多的是增量式的，但我认为这种增量式的过程，压倒性地，我认为将会是一个好消息的过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then even if it's much faster, it's also going to be a good news process.
zh: 即使它快得多，它也将是一个好消息的过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: It'll just be a good news process in the other way that I just described.
zh: 它只是以我刚才描述的另一种方式成为一个好消息的过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love hearing optimism and good news.
zh: 我喜欢听到乐观和好消息。

speaker: Unknown
en: I will also add that you've been... I was researching you ahead of this chat, and you've been right so many times about where the world is heading.
zh: 我还要补充一点，你一直在……我在这次聊天之前研究了你，关于世界的发展方向，你已经正确了很多次。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's why I'm especially excited to talk to you.
zh: 这就是为什么我特别兴奋能和你交谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll give you a short list.
zh: 我会给你列一个简短的清单。

speaker: Unknown
en: I imagine there are many more things.
zh: 我想还有更多的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So one, you were right about the Web and web browsers becoming important.
zh: 第一，关于 Web 和 Web 浏览器变得重要，你是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You were right about software eating the world.
zh: 关于软件正在吞噬世界，你是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Check.
zh: 确认。

speaker: Unknown
en: In 2011, you said that in 10 years, we're going to have 5 billion people using smartphones.
zh: 2011 年，你说在 10 年内，我们将有 50 亿人使用智能手机。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I believe the actual number ended up being six billion.
zh: 我相信实际数字最终是 60 亿。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, you had this debate with Peter Thiel that I came across, where you were debating whether technologies stop progressing or if new technology will continue to emerge.
zh: 另外，我发现了你与 Peter Thiel 的那场辩论，你们在辩论技术是停止进步了，还是新技术会继续涌现。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you were arguing there's progress.
zh: 你在论证有进步。

speaker: Unknown
en: Progress will continue.
zh: 进步会继续。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he was like, "No, I think we're done with cool technology."
zh: 而他就像说，“不，我认为我们用完了很酷的技术。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You were right.
zh: 你是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I imagine there are many more things you were right about.
zh: 我想还有更多你是对的的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So again, I love hearing your predictions because I feel like they're actually going to turn out to be correct.
zh: 所以再一次，我喜欢听到你的预测，因为我觉得它们实际上会变成正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was going to start by saying, I've been wrong about tons of things, but I buried those out back behind the shed.
zh: 我本来想先说，我在很多事情上错过，但我把它们埋在了棚屋后面。

speaker: Unknown
en: Delete them from the internet.
zh: 从互联网上删除它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: No browser can discount them.
zh: 没有浏览器可以打折它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, I have them nuked out of the internet archives so that they're never seen again.
zh: 是的，我已经把它们从互联网档案中核销掉了，这样它们就再也不会被看到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I'm wrong plenty of times also.
zh: 所以，我也错过了很多次。

speaker: Unknown
en: But yeah, look, I think some of those, I got right.
zh: 但是是的，听着，我想其中的一些，我是搞对了。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, I will say on the Peter one, I've come much more around to Peter's point of view.
zh: 顺便说一下，我要说关于 Peter 的那一点，我现在更倾向于 Peter 的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would probably argue that one quite a bit differently today than I did, and I would give his view, I think, a lot more credit.
zh: 今天，我可能会以相当不同的方式来争论那一点，我会给他的观点，我认为，更多的认可。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it actually goes to the discussion that we did, the conversation we just had, which is the... The real form of what Peter was arguing was we have lots of process in bit, we have lots of progress in bits, but we have very little progress in atoms.
zh: 这实际上回到了我们要做的讨论，我们刚才的对话，那就是……Peter 所争论的真正形式是我们在比特领域有很多进步，我们在比特领域有很多进展，但在原子领域进展甚微。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's the real core of what he was arguing.
zh: 这是他所论证的真正核心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think I was a little bit, I don't know, missing that or glossing that over a little bit because I was so focused on making sure people understood, "No, there actually is still progress happening in bits."
zh: 我认为我有点，我不知道，错过了这一点或者稍微忽略了一点，因为我太专注于确保人们理解，“不，在比特领域实际上仍有进展发生。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think a lot of his critiques around the lack of progress in atoms is real.
zh: 但我认为他关于原子领域缺乏进展的许多批评是真实的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, this goes back to this thing of like... And he's talked about this for a long time.
zh: 另外，这又回到了这个事情……而且他谈论这件事很久了。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the last 50 years, there has just been very little technological innovation in most of the economy.
zh: 在过去 50 年里，在经济的大部分领域，几乎没有任何技术创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's been very little technological innovation, in particular, anything involving atoms.
zh: 几乎没有任何技术创新，特别是任何涉及原子的技术创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's been very little real-world technological change.
zh: 几乎没有任何现实世界的技术变革。

speaker: Unknown
en: There just hasn't been.
zh: 只是确实没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: The built world is just not that different today than it was 50 years ago.
zh: 建成的世界今天并没有比 50 年前有太大的不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, if you contrast that, if you compare and contrast 1870 to 1930, it was a dramatically different world.
zh: 还有，如果你对比一下，如果你比较一下 1870 年到 1930 年，那是一个截然不同的世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you contrast 1930 to 1970, it was a dramatically different world.
zh: 如果对比 1930 年到 1970 年，那是一个截然不同的世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you contrast 1970 today, it's not that different.
zh: 如果对比 1970 年到现在，并没有太大的不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: And look, you just see that you could just walk around and it's just like, "Oh yeah, there's a bunch of buildings that were built in 1960, and there's a bridge that was built in 1930, and there's a dam that was built in like 1910, and there's a city that was founded in 1880."
zh: 而且，你看，你只要到处走走就会发现，“哦，对，有一群建于 1960 年的建筑，有一座建于 1930 年的桥，还有一座建于大约 1910 年的大坝，还有一个建于 1880 年的城市。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And like, "What have we done?
zh: 然后就像，“我们做了什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Where are our new cities?
zh: 我们的新城市在哪里？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where are new dams?
zh: 新大坝在哪里？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where's the California High-Speed Rail?
zh: 加州高铁在哪里？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's going on here?"
zh: 这里到底发生了什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I think he is right about a lot of that.
zh: 所以，我认为他在很多方面是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, this is also why I think that AI is not going to have as rapid... It's not going to be, again, this kind of utopian or dystopian view of everything changes overnight.
zh: 同样，这也是为什么我认为 AI 不会有那么快的……它不会，再次，那种一夜之间什么都改变的乌托邦或反乌托邦观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it just can't happen because of the reasons that Peter articulates, which is there's so much about how the world works that's basically just like wrapped up in red tape: like bureaucratic process, rules, restrictions, the politics.
zh: 我认为它不可能发生，因为 Peter 阐述的理由，也就是关于世界如何运作的很多东西基本上就是被繁文缛节包裹着：比如官僚流程、规则、限制、政治。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, unions, cartels, oligopolies, there's all these structures in the world that are economic or political or regulatory structures that basically prevent things from changing.
zh: 顺便说一下，工会、卡特尔、寡头垄断，世界上有所有这些结构，它们是经济或政治或监管结构，基本上阻止了事物的变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so let's take a great example: AI's impact on the healthcare system.
zh: 所以，让我们举一个很好的例子：AI 对医疗保健系统的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: By rights, AI is going to have a dramatic impact on the healthcare system, and in very positive ways.
zh: 理所当然，AI 将对医疗保健系统产生巨大影响，而且是以非常积极的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: But large parts of the medical system today, they are cartels.
zh: 但是，今天医疗体系的大部分，它们是卡特尔。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the doctors are a cartel, and nurses are a cartel, hospitals are a cartel.
zh: 所以医生是一个卡特尔，护士是一个卡特尔，医院是一个卡特尔。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then there's this push to nationalize all the healthcare systems, and then you've got a government monopoly.
zh: 然后有这种将所有医疗体系国有化的推动，然后你就拥有了政府垄断。

speaker: Unknown
en: And guess what cartels of monopolies don't like, is they don't like rapid change.
zh: 猜猜卡特尔垄断者不喜欢什么，他们不喜欢快速变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you show up as a kid and you're like, "Wow, I've got this new technology to do AI medicine."
zh: 所以你像个孩子一样出现，你说，“哇，我有这个做 AI 医学的新技术。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're like, "Oh, does it threaten doctor jobs?
zh: 然后他们说，“哦，它会威胁到医生的工作吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: In that case, we're going to block it."
zh: 既然如此，我们要阻止它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think a lot of consumers, by the way... I see this in my life, and you'll probably see this in your life also, which is ChatGPT is almost certainly a better doctor than your doctor today, but ChatGPT can't get a license to practice medicine.
zh: 而且我认为很多消费者，顺便说一下……我在我的生活中看到了这一点，你可能也会在你的生活中看到这一点，那就是 ChatGPT 几乎肯定比你今天的医生更好，但 ChatGPT 无法获得行医执照。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it can't substitute for a doctor.
zh: 所以，它不能替代医生。

speaker: Unknown
en: It can't prescribe medications.
zh: 它不能开药。

speaker: Unknown
en: It can't perform procedures.
zh: 它不能执行手术。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, there are these...
zh: 所以，存在这些……

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyway.
zh: 无论如何。

speaker: Unknown
en: So Peter, I think, was very articulate, and has been for a long time on like, "No, there are actually real structural impediments in the economy and in the political system that we have, that actually prevent..." The rates of change, that are anywhere near the rates of change that people have in the past.
zh: 所以 Peter，我认为，表达非常清楚，并且很长一段时间以来一直表达着，“不，经济和政治体系中确实存在真正的结构性障碍，实际上阻止了……”变化的速度，它接近不了人们过去拥有的变化速度。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can maybe say, optimistically, maybe the presence of the new magic technology of AI, maybe it causes us to revisit a lot of these assumptions for the first time in decades, to really say, "Okay, is this really the world we want to live in?
zh: 你也许可以说，乐观地看，也许 AI 这种新的魔法技术的出现，也许会让我们几十年来第一次重新审视许多这些假设，真正地说，“好吧，这真的是我们想要生活的世界吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't we actually want to get to the future faster?"
zh: 我们实际上难道不想更快地到达未来吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: So maybe, that would be the optimistic view.
zh: 所以也许，那将是乐观的观点。

speaker: Lenny
en: "It's time to build," somebody famously said. In my calendar, I actually have that as my... When I start to work, "It's time to build."
zh: “是时候建设了，”有人著名地说过。在我的日历上，我实际上把它作为我的……当我开始工作时，“是时候建设了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's my block in the morning of the day.
zh: 那是我早上的一天日程块。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you for that.
zh: 谢谢你提到了那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love the way you go from just macro to just like N-of-1, and I want to go to N-of-1.
zh: 我喜欢你这种从宏观直接跳转到个案（N-of-1）的方式，我也想谈谈个案。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of the listeners of this podcast are product managers, they're engineers, they're designers.
zh: 这个播客的很多听众是产品经理，他们是工程师，他们是设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of founders, but there's also a lot of non-founders.
zh: 有很多创始人，但也有很多非创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of people building product that aren't founders.
zh: 有很多人正在构建产品但他们不是创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And obviously, a lot of people are worried about where their career is going.
zh: 显然，很多人很担心他们的职业走向。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Is one of these roles going to disappear?"
zh: “这些角色中有一个会消失吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: "Is one of these roles are going to do really well?"
zh: “这些角色中有一个会做得很好吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: " How do I stay up to date?"
zh: “我如何保持最新？”

speaker: Unknown
en: You're close with a lot of teams, a lot of product teams.
zh: 你和很多团队很接近，很多产品团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's your sense of just the future of these three very specific roles: product manager, engineer, designer?
zh: 你对这三个非常具体的角色的未来有什么看法：产品经理、工程师、设计师？

speaker: Unknown
en: This, I think, is a really funny question.
zh: 这个，我认为，是一个很有趣的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: These three roles in particular, obviously, are the central roles for building for tech companies.
zh: 这三个角色，显然，是为科技公司构建的核心角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the way I've been describing it is... You know the concept of the Mexican standoff, right?
zh: 所以，我描述这件事的方式是……你知道墨西哥僵局这个概念，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Which is the movie scene where the two guys have guns point at each other's heads?
zh: 就是那种电影场景，两个人拿着枪指着对方的头？

speaker: Unknown
en: Mm-hmm.
zh: 嗯。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there's... If you watch John Woo movies, he loves to have... He does the three-way Mexican standoff, where you've got like a triangle, people.
zh: 然后……如果你看吴宇森的电影，他喜欢……他会搞三方墨西哥僵局，你有个三角形的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course, John Woo movies, they've got guns in both hands.
zh: 当然，吴宇森的电影里，他们双手都拿着枪。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, each is aiming at the other two and you've got this kind of standoff situation.
zh: 所以，每个人都瞄准另外两个人，你就有了这种僵局。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the way I've been describing this is there's like a Mexican standoff happening between those three roles: between product manager, designer, and coder.
zh: 我描述这件事的方式就是，在这三个角色之间发生了一场墨西哥僵局：在产品经理、设计师和程序员之间。

speaker: Unknown
en: Specifically, the following, which is every coder now believes they can also be a product manager and a designer because they have AI, every product manager thinks they can be a coder and a designer, and then every designer knows they can be a product manager and a coder.
zh: 具体来说，就是以下几点：现在每个程序员都相信自己也可以是产品经理和设计师，因为他们有 AI；每个产品经理都觉得自己可以是程序员和设计师；然后每个设计师都知道自己可以是产品经理和程序员。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, people in each of those roles now know or believe that with AI, they don't need the other two roles anymore.
zh: 因此，这些角色中的每个人现在都知道或相信，有了 AI，他们不再需要另外两个角色了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They can do that because they can have AI do that.
zh: 他们可以这么做，因为他们可以让 AI 去做那些事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then of course, there's the real irony, which is all three of them are going to realize that AI can also be a better manager.
zh: 然后当然，还有真正的讽刺之处，就是他们三个人都会意识到 AI 也可以是一个更好的管理者。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, they're going to be aiming the guns up the org chart, but that's the next phase.
zh: 所以，他们会把枪口指向组织架构图的上方，但那是下一阶段的事了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I think is so fascinating about this Mexican standoff is they're actually all kind of correct, I think.
zh: 我觉得这个墨西哥僵局最迷人的地方在于，我觉得他们其实都有点正确。

speaker: Unknown
en: Which is, AI is actually a pretty good... It's actually now a really good coder, it's actually now a really good designer, and it's also a really good product manager.
zh: 也就是说，AI 其实是个相当不错的……它现在确实是个很好的程序员，现在确实是个很好的设计师，也是个很好的产品经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's actually good at doing all three of those things, or at least doing a lot of the tasks involved in those three jobs.
zh: 它实际上擅长做这三件事，或者至少擅长做这三份工作中涉及的大量任务。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so again, this goes back to this idea of the super-empowered individual.
zh: 所以，这又回到了超级赋能个体的概念。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where if I'm a coder, step one is I need to make sure that I really understand AI coding, and what that means, and how coding is going to change in the future.
zh: 也就是说，如果我是个程序员，第一步是我需要确保我真正理解 AI 编程，这意味着什么，以及编程在未来会如何变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to understand specifically how to go from being a coder who writes code entirely by hand to being a coder who orchestrates a dozen instances of coding bots.
zh: 我需要具体理解如何从完全手写代码的程序员，转变为协调十几个编程机器人实例的程序员。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a change in the actual job of coding itself, which is happening right now.
zh: 编程工作本身的性质正在发生变化，这正在发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the other part of it is, "Okay, how do I become that super-empowered individual?
zh: 但另一部分是，“好吧，我如何成为那个超级赋能的个体？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do I become a coder that also then harnesses AI so that I can also be a great product manager, and I can also be a great designer?"
zh: 我如何成为一个利用 AI 的程序员，这样我也能成为一名伟大的产品经理，也能成为一名伟大的设计师？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the same thing for the product manager, which is, "How do I make sure that I can now use coding tools?
zh: 对于产品经理也是一样，就是，“我要如何确保我现在可以使用编码工具？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do I make sure I can also do AI-based design?"
zh: 我要如何确保我也能做基于 AI 的设计？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And the same thing for the designer, which is, "How do I use AI to also become a coder, and also become a product manager?"
zh: 对于设计师也是一样，就是“我如何使用 AI 也成为程序员，也成为产品经理？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then what you get is maybe, those individual roles change.
zh: 然后你得到的结果可能是，这些个人角色改变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe, those are not any more sort of stovepipe roles the way that they have been for the last 30 years or whatever.
zh: 也许，这些不再是像过去 30 年那样各自为政的角色了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But what happens is that the talented people in any of those roles become super powered, and they become good at doing all three of those things.
zh: 但发生的情况是，任何这些角色中的有才华的人变得超级强大，并且擅长做这三件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, those people become incredibly valuable, because then those are people who can actually build and design new products from scratch, which is the most valuable thing.
zh: 然后，这些人变得非常有价值，因为他们是那些能够真正从零开始构建和设计新产品的人，这是最有价值的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I think that's the opportunity.
zh: 所以，我认为这就是机会所在。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this answer.
zh: 我喜欢这个答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I'm hearing is, essentially, if you're amazing at any of these three roles, you will do well.
zh: 所以我听到的是，基本上，如果你在这三个角色中的任何一个都很出色，你会做得很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number one, if you're amazing at these roles, that's great.
zh: 第一，如果你在这些角色中表现出色，那很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: But also, part of being amazing in these roles is also being able to fully harness the new technology.
zh: 而且，在这些角色中表现出色的一部分也意味着能够充分利用新技术。

speaker: Unknown

en: By the way, you also really deeply want to understand how the AI itself works, because you want to... If people understand how the AI works, they're clearly able to get more value out of it than somebody who doesn't understand how it works.
zh: 顺便说一句，你也真的非常想理解 AI 本身是如何工作的，因为你想……如果人们理解 AI 是如何工作的，他们显然能够比那些不理解它如何工作的人从中获得更多价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're always more productive if you know how the machine works when you use the machine.
zh: 当你使用机器时，如果你知道它是如何工作的，你总是会更有效率。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the super-empowered individual on the other end of this that wants to do great things with the new technology, yes, you 100% want to understand this thing all the way down the stack because you want to be able to understand what it's giving you.
zh: 因此，在这条线另一端的想要利用新技术做大事的超级赋能个体，是的，你百分之百想要理解这东西直到堆栈底部，因为你想能够理解它给了你什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when something doesn't work or when something isn't right, you want to be able to really quickly understand why that is.
zh: 当某个东西不工作或者某个东西不对劲时，你想要能够真正快速地理解那是为什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, again, this goes back to education.
zh: 顺便说一句，同样，这又回到了教育。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is your best friend at helping you learn all of that because it's like, "Oh, I need to understand.
zh: AI 是你学习所有这一切的最好的朋友，因为它就像，“哦，我需要理解。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know, this isn't fast enough."
zh: 我不知道，这不够快。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to figure out... As a coder, I need to figure out how to do a different approach to memory management or something.
zh: 我需要弄清楚……作为一个编码员，我需要弄清楚如何做一种不同的内存管理方法之类的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can be like, "Well, shit.
zh: 然后你可以像，“好吧，该死。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't quite know how to do that.
zh: 我不太清楚怎么做那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, AI, let's spend 10 minutes.
zh: 好的，AI，让我们花 10 分钟。

speaker: Unknown
en: Teach me how to do this.
zh: 教我怎么做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Teach me what this all means."
zh: 教我这都意味着什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So all of a sudden, you have this incredibly synergistic relationship with the AI, where it's also helping you get better at the same time that's doing a lot of work for you.
zh: 所以突然间，你与 AI 有了这种令人难以置信的协同关系，它也在为你做大量工作的同时帮助你变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, I was going to say, I was a big Pearl programmer.
zh: 顺便说一句，我本来想说，我是个大的 Pearl 程序员。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was an engineer for 10 years, and that was my language of choice.
zh: 我当了 10 年工程师，那是我选择的语言。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you remember?
zh: 你记得吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know when you were doing it, but do you remember... At least early on, did you ever hit this where C coders were looking down their nose at you and being like-
zh: 我不知道你是什么时候做的，但是你记得……至少在早期，你有没有遇到过这种情况，C 程序员看不起你，比如说——

speaker: Unknown
en: For sure.
zh: 肯定的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was like, "This is so slow.
zh: 就像是，“这太慢了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not going to scale.
zh: 它无法扩展。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are you spending all your time on this thing?"
zh: 你把所有时间都花在这东西上干什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, exactly.
zh: 是的，正是。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course, and again, it started this thing where they were sort of correct.
zh: 当然，同样，这开启了这样一件事，他们某种程度上是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Which is, at the beginning, it wasn't fast enough or whatever.
zh: 也就是，在开始时，它不够快或者不管怎样。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the end, they were definitely wrong, which is it got much better, much faster.
zh: 到了最后，他们肯定是错的，也就是它变得更好、更快了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it swept the world.
zh: 它横扫了世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most coding today happens as scripting languages.
zh: 如今大多数编码发生在脚本语言上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then by the way, along the way, the people who really understood the scripting languages and the people who understood all the lower level systems, they were the ones who were able to actually make the scripting languages actually work really well.
zh: 然后顺便说一句，在这个过程中，那些真正理解脚本语言的人和那些理解所有底层系统的人，他们是那些能够让脚本语言真正良好工作的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, that was a great example of this kind of adaptation.
zh: 所以，这是这种适应性的一个很好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, the result of that was a far higher number of people writing code with scripting languages than were ever writing code with lower level languages.
zh: 同样，那的结果是使用脚本语言写代码的人的数量远远多于曾经使用底层语言写代码的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think this will just be a more dramatic version of that.
zh: 而且我认为这只是那件事的一个更戏剧化的版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that Pearl was designed by a linguist.
zh: 我喜欢 Pearl 是由一位语言学家设计的这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if you remember that part.
zh: 我不知道你是否记得那部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's what made it so nice to code with.
zh: 而这就是为什么用它编码是如此令人愉快。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's funny because, of course, it was so notorious for being impossible to understand.
zh: 这很有趣，因为当然，它以不可能理解而臭名昭著。

speaker: Unknown
en: How ironic.
zh: 真是讽刺。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by Datadog, now home to Eppo, the leading experimentation and feature flagging platform.
zh: 本期节目由 Datadog 为您带来，现在是 Eppo 的所在地，Eppo 是领先的实验和特性标记平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: Product managers at the world's best companies use Datadog, the same platform their engineers rely on every day to connect product insights to product issues like bugs, UX friction, and business impact.
zh: 世界上最好的公司的产品经理使用 Datadog，这是他们的工程师每天依赖的同一个平台，用于将产品洞察与产品问题（如 Bug、UX 摩擦和业务影响）连接起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: It starts with product analytics where PMs can watch replays, review funnels, dive into retention, and explore their growth metrics.
zh: 它始于产品分析，产品经理可以在其中观看回放、查看漏斗、深入研究留存率并探索他们的增长指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where other tools stop, Datadog goes even further.
zh: 在其他工具停止的地方，Datadog 走得更远。

speaker: Unknown
en: It helps you actually diagnose the impact of funnel drop-offs, and bugs, and UX friction.
zh: 它帮助你实际诊断漏斗流失、Bug 和 UX 摩擦的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: Once you know where to focus, experiments prove what works.
zh: 一旦你知道了关注点在哪里，实验就会证明什么有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: I saw this firsthand when I was at Airbnb, where our experimentation platform was critical for analyzing what worked and where things went wrong.
zh: 我在 Airbnb 时亲眼看到了这一点，我们的实验平台对于分析什么有效以及哪里出了问题至关重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the same team that built the experimentation at Airbnb built Eppo.
zh: 同样在 Airbnb 构建实验平台的团队构建了 Eppo。

speaker: Unknown
en: Datadog then lets you go beyond the numbers with session replay.
zh: 然后，Datadog 让你通过会话回放超越数字本身。

speaker: Unknown
en: Watch exactly how users interact with heat maps and scroll maps to truly understand their behavior.
zh: 通过热力图和滚动图准确观察用户如何互动，以真正理解他们的行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: And all of this is powered by feature flags that are tied to real-time data, so that you can roll out safely, target precisely, and learn continuously.
zh: 所有这些都由与实时数据绑定的功能标志提供支持，以便你可以安全地推出、精准地定位并持续学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: Datadog is more than engineering metrics.
zh: Datadog 不仅仅是工程指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's where great product teams learn faster, think smarter, and ship with confidence.
zh: 它是优秀产品团队学得更快、思考更聪明、并有信心发布的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Request a demo at datadoghq.com/lenny.
zh: 在 datadoghq.com/lenny 申请演示。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's datadoghq.com/lenny.
zh: 网址是 datadoghq.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Coming back to this kind of triad, the other element that I hear more and more of is just the skill of taste, and design, and user experience, it feels like that's a very hard skill to learn.
zh: 回到这种三位一体，我听到的另一个越来越多的要素是品味、设计和用户体验的技能，这感觉是一门非常难学的技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And to me, it tells me design is going to be much more valuable in the future.
zh: 对我来说，这告诉我设计在未来将具有更高的价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that's right.
zh: 是的，没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, here, this is a great example.
zh: 而且，在这里，这又是一个很好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: So again, the task level of, like, "Design the perfect icon," is going to be, all right, the AI's going to do that all day long.
zh: 所以，同样，像“设计完美的图标”这样的任务层面，将会是，AI 会整天做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: If it gives you a thousand icon designs, it's going to be great.
zh: 如果它给你一千个图标设计，那会很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to be fantastic, whatever.
zh: 那会很棒，不管怎样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, there will still be some level of human icon design or whatever, but AI is going to get really good at that.
zh: 顺便说一句，仍然会有某种程度的人工图标设计或别的什么，但 AI 会非常擅长那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: But what are we trying to do, kind of capital D design of, like, "All right, what is this thing for, and how is this going to function in a world of human beings?
zh: 但是我们试图做的是某种大写 D 的设计，比如“好吧，这东西是干什么的，以及它将如何在人类世界中运作？

speaker: Unknown
en: And is this going to make people happy when they use it?
zh: 它会让人们在使用它时感到快乐吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this going to make people feel good about themselves?
zh: 它会让人们对自己感觉良好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it going to fit into the rest of their life?
zh: 它会融入他们生活的其余部分吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it going to, I don't know, challenge them in the right way?"
zh: 它会，我不知道，以正确的方式挑战他们吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: All of these kinds of higher level questions that the great designers have always thought about.
zh: 所有这些都是伟大的设计师一直在思考的这类更高级别的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: The job of designer will involve much more of those higher level, more important components, and then again, with AI doing a lot more of the underlying tasks.
zh: 设计师的工作将涉及更多这些更高级别、更重要的组成部分，然后同样，由 AI 做更多的底层任务。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so one way to think about it is, I don't know, you think of the world's best designers, Jony Ive or whatever, and you could be like, "Wow."
zh: 所以思考这个问题的一种方式是，我不知道，你想到世界上最好的设计师，Jony Ive 或什么的，你可以像，“哇。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Like, if I'm a designer today, if I'm a 25-year-old designer and I aspire to be Jony Ive in a decade, it's all of a sudden, I have a new path that I can use to get there, which is... Because Jony did everything.
zh: 就像，如果我是今天的设计师，如果我是 25 岁的设计师，并且我立志在十年内成为 Jony Ive，突然间，我有了一条可以到达那里的新路径，那就是…… 因为 Jony 做了一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: He did it without AI.
zh: 他在没有 AI 的情况下做到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, a young designer tends to be like, "Wow, if I really harness AI in a decade, I'm going to be like the best designer of the world's ever seen because it's not just going to be me.
zh: 现在，年轻的设计师往往会想，“哇，如果我在十年内真正利用 AI，我将成为世界上有史以来最好的设计师，因为不仅仅是我。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to be me, plus being so super empowered by this technology to be able to do so much more.
zh: 将是我，加上被这项技术超级赋能，能够做更多的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then so much more of my time and attention is going to be able to be focused on these higher-level things that most designers never get to."
zh: 然后我的时间和注意力中有更多的一部分将能够专注于这些大多数设计师从未涉及的高级事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's going to be another great example of that.
zh: 我认为这将是另一个很好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: So maybe what I'm hearing here is kind of this T-shaped strategy of if you want to be successful in any three of these roles, be very, very, very good at that specific role: product management, engineering design.
zh: 所以也许我在这里听到的是一种 T 型策略，如果你想在这三个角色中的任何一个中取得成功，就要在那个特定角色上非常、非常、非常出色：产品管理、工程设计。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then get good enough at these other two roles.
zh: 然后在另外两个角色上做得足够好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's great.
zh: 我认为这很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's really relevant.
zh: 我认为这真的很相关。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then Scott Adamson, firstly, just passed away, which is a real tragedy.
zh: 然后 Scott Adamson，首先，刚刚去世了，这真是一个悲剧。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I referred for years to, actually, Scott Adams.
zh: 但我多年来实际上指的是 Scott Adams。

speaker: Unknown
en: He had this famous career advice he would give people, which I think makes a lot of sense.
zh: 他有他给人们的著名职业建议，我认为非常有道理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Which dovetails with what you're saying, which-
zh: 这与你所说的相吻合，这-

speaker: Unknown
en: ... advice he would give people which I think makes a lot of sense, which dovetails with what you're saying, which is he used to say it's like, look, he said, "I could have been a pretty good cartoonist or I could have been pretty good at business, but the fact that I was a cartoonist who understood business made me spectacularly great at making Dilbert."
zh: ……他给人们的建议我认为非常有道理，这与你所说的相吻合，那就是他过去常说，看，他说，“我本可以成为一个相当好的漫画家，或者我本可以在商业上做得相当好，但我是理解商业的漫画家这一事实，让我在制作 Dilbert 方面变得极其出色。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Because even the world's best cartoonist who didn't understand business could have never written Dilbert, and then the world's best business people who didn't know how to do cartoons couldn't have done Dilbert.
zh: 因为即使是世界上最不理解商业的最好的漫画家也永远写不出 Dilbert，而世界上最不会画漫画的商业人士也做不出 Dilbert。

speaker: Unknown
en: It took somebody who actually had both of those skills to be able to make Dilbert which is one of the most successful cartoons in history.
zh: 需要一个实际上同时拥有这两种技能的人才能制作出 Dilbert，这是历史上最成功的漫画之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the way Scott always described it was that from a career development standpoint, the additive effect of being good at two things is more than double.
zh: 所以 Scott 一直描述的方式是，从职业发展的角度来看，擅长两件事的叠加效应比翻倍还要多。

speaker: Unknown
en: The additive effect of being good at three things is more than triple because you become a super relevant specialist in the combination of the domains, and, look, I mean, you see this all over the economy.
zh: 擅长三件事的叠加效应比翻三倍还要多，因为你成为了领域组合中的超级相关专家，而且，看，我的意思是，你在整个经济中都能看到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, you see this all over the economy, but I'll give you an example.
zh: 我的意思是，你在整个经济中都能看到这一点，但我给你举个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hollywood, just Hollywood as an example.
zh: 好莱坞，就以好莱坞为例。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are a lot of writers who can't direct a movie and they can be very successful writers.
zh: 有很多作家不会导演电影，但他们可以成为非常成功的作家。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are a lot of directors who can't write a movie.
zh: 有很多导演不会写电影剧本。

speaker: Unknown
en: They can be very successful directors.
zh: 他们可以成为非常成功的导演。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the superstars in the entertainment industry are the people who can write and direct.
zh: 但娱乐界的超级明星是那些既能写又能导的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't have a term for those.
zh: 他们没有针对那些人的术语。

speaker: Unknown
en: They call us auteurors, and those are the people who are the real creative forces that move the field.
zh: 他们称我们为 auteurors，那些人是推动该领域的真正的创造力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so again, and by the way, Hollywood, actually it's really funny, I've been spending a lot of time talking to Hollywood people about AI.
zh: 所以再一次，顺便说一下，好莱坞，其实这真的很有趣，我花了很多时间和好莱坞的人谈论 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hollywood has the same Mexican stand-off going right now that we describe in tech, except in Hollywood, for example, for filmmaking, it's the director, it's the writer, and the actor.
zh: 好莱坞目前正在进行我们在科技界描述的那种墨西哥僵局，除了在好莱坞，例如，对于电影制作来说，它是导演、编剧和演员。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because the director is now thinking, "Wow, I don't need the writer anymore because the AI can write the script and I don't need the actor anymore because I can have AI actors."
zh: 因为导演现在在想，“哇，我不再需要编剧了，因为 AI 可以写剧本，我也不再需要演员了，因为我可以有 AI 演员。”

speaker: Unknown
en: The writer is saying, "Well, I don't need the director because I can direct the movie and the AI can do the actors."
zh: 编剧在说，“好吧，我不需要导演，因为我可以导演电影，而 AI 可以做演员的工作。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And the actor is saying, "I don't need either one of these guys.
zh: 演员在说，“这两个家伙我一个都不需要。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I can have the AI direct the thing, I can have the AI write the thing and I'm just going to show up and do my performance."
zh: 我可以让 AI 来导演这个东西，我可以让 AI 来写这个东西，而我只需要出现并完成我的表演。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's the same kind of triangular configuration, and again, what's great about it is they're all correct.
zh: 所以这是同样的三角构型，同样，它的美妙之处在于他们都是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Each person in each of those three fields is going to be able to expand laterally and pick up those additional skills, and then as a consequence, you're going to have more people who can write and direct or write and act or direct and act or do all three.
zh: 这三个领域中每个领域的人都将能够横向扩展并掌握那些额外的技能，因此，你将会有更多的人能够编写和导演，或编写和表演，或导演和表演，或者三者都做。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think to your point, your T-shaped thing, I think that's going to be true basically across the entire economy.
zh: 我认为说到你的观点，你的 T 型理论，我认为这基本上将在整个经济中成为现实。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you think about the T, if you think about the T configuration, it's like, yeah, the breadth, the top of the T is like how many individual domains are you familiar enough with to be able to use the AI tools to be able to do really good work.
zh: 如果你考虑这个 T，如果你考虑 T 型构型，就像，是的，广度，T 的顶部就像你熟悉多少个独立的领域，以便能够使用 AI 工具来做非常好的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then this part of the T is how deep can you go in at least one of those domains so that you really, really deeply know what you're doing.
zh: 而 T 的这部分是你在至少其中一个领域能够深入到什么程度，以至于你真正、真正深刻地知道你在做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you're super deep on coding and you can use AI to do design and you can use AI to do product management, that's your T right there, and you're a triple threat at the top of the T, but with this level of technical grounding underneath that.
zh: 但如果你在编码方面非常深入，你可以使用 AI 做设计，你可以使用 AI 做产品管理，那就是你的 T 型，你是 T 型顶部的三重威胁，但在那之下有这种程度的技术基础。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, at that point, again, you're the super-powered individual, you're going to be able to just perform like sheets of magic, for example, in terms of designing and building your products that people in my generation couldn't have even dreamed of.
zh: 我的意思是，在那个时候，再一次，你是超级强力的个体，你将能够像施展魔法一样表现，例如，在设计和构建你的产品方面，我这一代人甚至无法梦想。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that this is a universal kind of theory that I think can apply across the entire economy.
zh: 所以我认为这是一个通用的理论，我认为可以应用于整个经济。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to invent a new framework right now.
zh: 我现在要发明一个新的框架。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, forget the T framework.
