speaker: Unknown
en: Bitcoin is probably the most spectacular example with Ethereum right behind it which wasn't quite one person but a very small team.
zh: Bitcoin 可能是最壮观的例子，Ethereum 紧随其后，虽然不是一个人，但是一个非常小的团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: You had Instagram and WhatsApp that had very big outcomes with very small teams.
zh: 你有 Instagram 和 WhatsApp，它们以非常小的团队获得了巨大的成果。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every once in a while you get one of these things where you just, something hits, and you just have a very small number of people associated with it.
zh: 偶尔你会得到这样的东西，你只是，某个东西成功了，而你只有非常少的人与之相关。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that said, most software companies obviously end up with huge numbers of employees.
zh: 话虽如此，大多数软件公司显然最终都会拥有大量的员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think the most leading-edge founders are thinking of, okay, how do I reconstitute the actual very definition or idea of having a company and can you have a company that's literally basically just all AI.
zh: 所以我认为最前沿的创始人在考虑，好吧，我如何重组拥有一个公司的实际定义或想法，你能拥有一家基本上完全由 AI 组成的公司吗。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're doing anything in the real world, that's hard, but if you're doing software, that seems like it might be feasible in some cases.
zh: 如果你在现实世界中做任何事情，这很难，但如果你在做软件，在某些情况下这似乎是可行的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there's the ultimate example of that which is can you have like autonomous AI economy stuff happening where you have AI bots on the blockchain or something that are basically out there functioning as a business and making money and just literally where the AI does all the work itself and just issues me dividends.
zh: 然后这里有终极的例子，就是你能否拥有自主的 AI 经济事物发生，比如你在区块链上有 AI 智能体或类似的东西，它们基本上在那里作为一个企业运作并赚钱，实际上就是 AI 自己做所有的工作，然后只给我分红。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe that's the final outlier result.
zh: 也许那是最终的异常结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have a few founders who are chasing that kind of thing.
zh: 我们有一些创始人在追求那种东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I would describe that as kind of the latter that the best founders are on.
zh: 所以我会把它描述为最优秀的创始人所处的那个阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: Super interesting.
zh: 超级有意思。

speaker: Unknown
en: This whole idea of a one-person billion-dollar company, I think it depends on your definition of what this is, like an outcome I could see.
zh: 这种一人十亿美元公司的整个想法，我认为这取决于你对这是什么定义，比如一个结果我能看到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Running my newsletter as one person with some contractors, there's so many little annoying things that I have to deal with, with just support tickets and issues and bugs.
zh: 我作为一个人带着一些承包商经营我的通讯，我有这么多必须处理的小麻烦事，只有支持工单、问题和 Bug。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's hard for me to imagine actually a one-person billion-dollar company, even if AI is handling so much of your support because there's just so many random-edge cases that I'm just... like filling out forms.
zh: 我很难想象真正的一人十亿美元公司，即使 AI 处理了你这么多的支持工作，因为有这么多随机的边缘情况，我只是……比如填写表格。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I guess depends on, do you have contractors, does that count, what does it mean to be a one person.
zh: 所以我想这取决于，你有承包商吗，那算不算，成为一个人意味着什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I'm just like, "I can't see that happening."
zh: 但我只是说，“我看不到这种情况发生。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, look, Bitcoin, Satoshi pulled it off.
zh: 我的意思是，看，Bitcoin，Satoshi 做到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the open source community now, does that count?
zh: 但是现在的开源社区，那算吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I guess it counts.
zh: 我想这算数。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, exactly.
zh: 是的，确实。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, I would say I don't propose to have answers here, but more just like the smartest people I know or many of the smartest people I know are thinking hard about this.
zh: 所以是的，我想说我不打算在这里给出答案，但更多的是像我认识的许多最聪明的人正在对此进行深入思考。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you think about moats?
zh: 你们怎么看护城河？

speaker: Unknown
en: A big question constantly in AI, the fact that everything's changing, just what's your guys' thesis on moats in AI?
zh: AI 中一个不断出现的大问题，事实上一切都在变化，你们对 AI 中的护城河有什么论题？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that even a thing?
zh: 这甚至是个东西吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you care?
zh: 你们在乎吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: My experience with really big technological transformations, and of course, I kind of lived this directly with the internet and I saw this happen, is the really big technological transformations, they take a long time to play out and there's all of these structural implications that just kind of cascade out over time.
zh: 我对真正的大型技术变革的经验，当然，我直接经历了互联网时代，我亲眼目睹了这一切，是真正的大型技术变革，它们需要很长时间才能展开，并且有所有的这些结构性影响随着时间推移而层层涌现。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this rush to judgment upfront where people say, "Oh, it's therefore obvious that X, Y, Z. It's therefore obvious that this kind of company is going to be the company of the future, not that kind.
zh: 一开始会有这种急于下结论的情况，人们会说，“噢，因此 X、Y、Z 是显而易见的。这种类型的公司显然将是未来的公司，而不是那种。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's obvious that this incumbent's going to be able to adapt and this other one isn't.
zh: 显然这个现有企业将能够适应，而另一个则不能。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's obvious that there's economic opportunity in this kind of startup and not in these others.
zh: 显然这种类型的初创公司有经济机会，而那些其他的没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's obvious that the moats are going to be in this area of the technology, but not in this other area."
zh: 显然护城河将位于技术的这一领域，而不是那一领域。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What everybody does is they kind of state those things with just an enormous amount of self-assurance where they really sound like they have all the answers.
zh: 每个人所做的就是他们以极大的自信陈述这些事情，听起来真的好像他们拥有所有答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then what happens is these ideas kind of saturate the media because the media naturally prizes definitive answers over open questions because it... it's like when CNBC is booking guests, they want a guest who's going to come on and say, "Yes, this is the way, it's going to be X."
zh: 然后发生的是这些想法往往充斥着媒体，因为媒体自然地重视确定的答案而不是开放的问题，因为……这就像当 CNBC 预约嘉宾时，他们想要一个能上节目说“是的，这就是道路，它将是 X”的嘉宾。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not like, "You know, I think that's a really good question and let's debate it from eight different angles."
zh: 而不是，“你知道，我认为这是一个非常好的问题，让我们从八个不同的角度来辩论它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What I found is if you look back on those predictions a few years later, and you can do this by the way, if you pull up coverage of the internet from 1993 through 1997, or for that matter even through 2005 or 2010, and you look at the kinds of confidence statements people were making in the first 10 or 15 years, I would say almost all of them were wrong, generally quite badly wrong.
zh: 我发现的是，如果你在几年后回顾这些预测，顺便说一下，你可以这样做，如果你调出 1993 年到 1997 年关于互联网的报道，或者甚至到 2005 年或 2010 年的报道，并且看看人们在头 10 年或 15 年里所做的那些自信声明，我会说几乎所有的都是错的，通常是非常错误的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think the process, I think there's going to be a massive amount of technological change.
zh: 所以我认为这个过程，我认为将会有大量的技术变革。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to be like, I don't know, five or six layers of structural change that will play out over time.
zh: 这将会有，我不知道，五或六层的结构性变化将随着时间的推移而展开。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, we've talked about a lot of this, but the implications on what are the definition of products, what are the definitions of companies, what are the definitions of jobs, what are the definitions of industries.
zh: 而且，我们已经讨论了很多这方面，但是对于产品定义、公司定义、工作定义、行业定义的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: How does this play out at the national level?
zh: 这在国家层面如何展开？

speaker: Unknown
en: How does this play out at the global level?
zh: 这在全球层面如何展开？

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, how does this intersect with politics?
zh: 顺便说一下，这如何与政治交汇？

speaker: Unknown
en: How does this intersect with unions?
zh: 这如何与工会交汇？

speaker: Unknown
en: How does this intersect with war?
zh: 这如何与战争交汇？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's China going to do?
zh: 中国会做什么？ [[829] ] 所以有巨大的未知数，非常非常大量的未知数，我认为预先判断这些事情是非常、非常危险的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so there are just a tremendous number of unknowns, a very, very large number of unknowns, and I think it's just like really, really dangerous to prejudge these things.
zh: 所以有海量的未知因素，非常、非常多的未知因素，我认为预先判断这些事情真的是真的非常危险。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll just run this as a thought experiment and you can see what you think on this, but it's like, are AI models themselves defensible.
zh: 我这就把它作为一个思维实验来讲，你可以看看你怎么想，但这就像，AI 模型本身是可防御的吗。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there a moat on AI models?
zh: AI 模型上有护城河吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And on the one hand, you'd be like, "Wow, it certainly seems like there is or should be," because if something takes billions of dollars to build and you need this incredible critical mass of computing data and there's only a certain number of engineers in the world that know how to do this and they are getting paid like MBA stars.
zh: 一方面，你会像，“哇，看起来确实有或者应该有，”因为如果某种东西花费数十亿美元来构建，并且你需要这种不可思议的算力和数据的临界质量，而且世界上只有一定数量的工程师知道如何做这件事，并且他们得到的报酬就像 MBA 明星一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then these companies have to deal with all these crazy political issues and press issues and reputational stuff and regulatory and legal.
zh: 然后这些公司必须处理所有这些疯狂的政治问题和新闻问题和声誉问题以及监管和法律问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: All of that translates to, okay, probably at the end of this, there's going to be two or three companies that are going to end up with like 100%, I don't know, whatever, 50/50 or 30/30/30 or 90/10 and one, or whatever it is, market share and then they're going to have whatever profitability they have and it's going to be kind of a classic oligopoly, or maybe one company's going to win definitively and it'll be a monopoly.
zh: 所有这些都转化为，好吧，大概在这结束时，将会有两三家公司最终拥有，我不知道， whatever，50/50 或 30/30/30 或 90/10 和 1，或者 whatever 它是，市场份额，然后他们将拥有 whatever 利润他们拥有，并且这将是一种经典的寡头垄断，或者也许一家公司将明确获胜并成为垄断。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, those outcomes have happened in software many times before.
zh: 顺便说一下，这些结果在软件中发生过很多次。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so maybe that will be the outcome.
zh: 所以也许那将是结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other side of it is if you had told me three years ago that in the Christmas of ChatGPT that within basically a year to year and a half there would be five other American companies that would have basically exactly capable products, and then there would be another five companies out of China that would have exactly capable products, and then there would additionally be open source that was basically the same, I would have been like, "Wow, the thing that seemed like it was black magic all of a sudden has become like commoditized really fast," which by the way, is exactly what happened.
zh: 它的另一面是，如果你在三年前告诉我，在 ChatGPT 的那个圣诞节，在基本上一年到一年半的时间内，会有其他五家美国公司拥有基本完全有能力的产品，然后会有来自中国的另外五家公司拥有完全有能力的产品，然后此外还有开源基本上相同，我会像，“哇，这个看起来像黑魔法的东西突然间变得非常商品化，”顺便说一下，这正是发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Within a year of GPT3 coming out, there were there open source GPT3s running on a fraction of the hardware that were available for free.
zh: 在 GPT3 出来的一年内，有开源的 GPT3 在一小部分硬件上运行，可以免费获得。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there were five.
zh: 然后有五个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you've got, fully in the game, you've got Google and you've got Anthropic and you've got xAI and you've got Meta and you've got all these other companies that are... and then DeepSeek and Kimi and all these other Chinese companies.
zh: 现在你已经完全参与其中，你有 Google 和你有 Anthropic 和你有 xAI 和你有 Meta 和你有所有这些其他公司……然后 DeepSeek 和 Kimi 和所有这些其他中国公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so even at the level of LLMs or AI models, you can squint and make that argument either way.
zh: 所以即使在 LLM 或 AI 模型的层面上，你也可以眯起眼睛从任一方面做出那个论点。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, same thing at the level of apps.
zh: 顺便说一下，在应用程序层面上也是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like one school of thought is apps are not a thing because the model's just going to do everything, but another way of looking at it is no, actually adapting the model is kind of the engine into a domain involving human beings where you need to actually have it fit for purpose to be able to function in the medical industry or the legal industry or whatever or coding.
zh: 就像一种思想流派是应用程序不是个东西，因为模型将做所有事情，但另一种看待方式是不，实际上使模型适应涉及人类的领域，你需要真正适合目的以便能够在医疗行业或法律行业或 whatever 或编码中运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: No, you actually need the application level's actually going to matter enormously, and maybe the LLMs commoditize and maybe the value goes to the apps.
zh: 不，你实际上需要应用程序层面实际上将非常重要，也许 LLM 商品化，也许价值转移到应用程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, you can kind of squint either way on that one, and I know very smart people who are on both sides of that argument.
zh: 而且，你可以对那个问题眯起眼睛从任一方面看，我知道非常聪明的人在那个争论的双方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so my honest answer on this is I think we're in a process of discovery over time.
zh: 所以我对此的诚实回答是我认为我们正在一个随时间推移的发现过程中。

speaker: Unknown
en: The way I think about this kind of structurally is it's a complex adaptive system.
zh: 我从结构上思考这一种的方式是它是一个复杂的适应系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: The technology itself provides one of the inputs.
zh: 技术本身提供输入之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: The legal and regulatory process is another input.
zh: 法律和监管过程是另一个输入。

speaker: Unknown
en: Actual individual choices made by entrepreneurs matter a lot.
zh: 创业者做出的实际个人选择很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: The economics matter a lot.
zh: 经济状况很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Availability of investor capital varies over time, that matters a lot.
zh: 投资者资本的可用性随时间变化，这很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a complex system, and so we actually don't know the outcomes on this yet.
zh: 这是一个复杂的系统，所以我们实际上还不知道这方面的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need to be open to surprises at the structural level of what happens.
zh: 我们需要对结构层面发生的事情保持开放态度，以面对意外。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course, as a VC, this is very exciting because it means we're doing this now.
zh: 当然，作为一名 VC，这非常令人兴奋，因为这意味着我们现在就在做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: We should make bets along every one of these strategies and see how this plays out.
zh: 我们应该沿着这些策略中的每一个方向下注，看看事情如何发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would just say, there may be, I don't know, there may be like one particularly brilliant, I don't know, hedge fund manager or something who has this all figured out, but I guess I would say if they exist I haven't met them yet.
zh: 我只想说，可能有一个，我不知道，可能有一个特别聪明的，我不知道，对冲基金经理或类似的人已经把这一切都弄清楚了，但我想我会说，如果这样的人存在，我还没有见过他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I'm hearing here is don't over-obsess with moats at this point because we have no idea what'll end up being, and as much as it may feel like, okay, there's no way OpenAI will lose this lead, clearly we're seeing a lot of competition.
zh: 所以我在这里听到的是，这个时候不要过度痴迷于护城河，因为我们不知道最终会是什么样，尽管感觉上好像，好吧，OpenAI 不可能失去这个领先优势，但显然我们看到很多竞争。

speaker: Unknown
en: GPT wrapper point is really great.
zh: GPT wrapper 这个观点真的很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was such a derogatory term, I don't know, a year ago, just like, "You're just a GPT wrapper."
zh: 这曾经是一个贬义词，我不知道，一年前，就像是，“你只是一个 GPT wrapper。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now it's like the companies that are the biggest companies, the fastest growing companies in the world.
zh: 现在那些最大的公司，世界上增长最快的公司，就是这样的公司。

speaker: Marc Andreessen
en: Yeah. Well, it's like a little bit like, I don't know, I mean, even just like with... this has been the holiday, three years ago was the holiday of ChatGPT. This last month or whatever has been the holiday of Claude, particularly Claude Code for coding. But it's pretty amazing because it's like, okay, there was Claude which is obviously a great accomplishment, but then there's Claude Code which is an app. It's a Claude wrapper. It's agent harness. And then they did this amazing thing where they came out with, was it Coworker?
zh: 是的。嗯，这有点像，我不知道，我是说，甚至就像……这是节日，三年前是 ChatGPT 的节日。上个月或者不管是什么时候是 Claude 的节日，特别是用于编程的 Claude Code。但这非常神奇，因为就像，好吧，有 Claude，这显然是一个伟大的成就，但还有 Claude Code，它是一个应用程序。它是一个 Claude wrapper。它是一个智能体驾驭工具。然后他们做了这件惊人的事情，他们推出了，那是 Coworker 吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Cowork.
zh: Cowork。

speaker: Unknown
en: Cowork.
zh: Cowork。

speaker: Unknown
en: And remember what they said of Cowork, which is Claude Code worked Cowork in a week.
zh: 还记得他们对 Cowork 的评价，也就是 Claude Code 在一周内做出了 Cowork。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, a week and a half, yep, 100%.
zh: 是的，一周半，是的，百分之百。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, and there's two ways looking at that which is like, "Wow, that's really..." I mean, obviously that's really impressive that Claude Code was able to build Cowork in a week and a half.
zh: 看待这件事有两种方式，就像，“哇，这真的……”我的意思是，显然 Claude Code 能够在一周半内构建出 Cowork，这真的很令人印象深刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's great.
zh: 这很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's amazing.
zh: 这很神奇。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other way to look at it is Cowork was developed in a week and a half.
zh: 另一种看待它的方式是 Cowork 是在一周半内开发出来的。

speaker: Unknown
en: How much complexity could there be?
zh: 能有多复杂呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: How much of a barrier to entry can there be in something that was developed in a week and a half?
zh: 在一周半内开发出来的东西，准入门槛能有多高？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then again, it's this push and this pull thing where it's like, wow, it's incredibly functional, incredibly valuable, and people all over the world and every day now are like, "Wow, I can't believe what I can do with this.
zh: 然后又一次，这是这种推和拉的事情，就像，哇，它非常有功能性，非常有价值，世界各地的人们现在每天都在说，“哇，我不敢相信我能用这个做事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like the most magical product ever."
zh: 这就像是有史以来最神奇的产品。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But at the same time, it took a week and a half.
zh: 但与此同时，它只花了一周半。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so every other model company, I'm sure, you'd have to expect, is sitting there being like, "Okay, obviously we need to build an Asian artist and then obviously we need to build a Cowork thing for regular people."
zh: 因此，每一家其他的模型公司，我敢肯定，你必须得预料到，正坐在那里想，“好的，显然我们需要构建一个 Asian artist（注：疑为艺术家相关产品），然后显然我们需要为普通大众构建一个 Cowork 这样的东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not even saying I know anything, but just obviously they're all going to do that.
zh: 我甚至不是说我了解什么内情，但显然他们都会这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so how defensible is that?
zh: 那么这有多大可防御性呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: In six months, and we've seen this happen before, is Claude Code going to get lapped the same way that GitHub Copilot got lapped?
zh: 在六个月内，我们以前见过这种情况发生，Claude Code 会被超过去吗，就像 GitHub Copilot 被超过去一样？

speaker: Unknown
en: The history in the last three years has been everything that looks like it's like the fundamental breakthrough gets basically replicated and lapped very quickly.
zh: 过去三年的历史是，任何看起来像是根本性突破的东西都会很快被复制和超越。

speaker: Unknown
en: Many of the smartest people I know in the field, when I really talk to them, kind of get a couple drinks into them, they're like, "Yeah."
zh: 我认识的这个领域许多最聪明的人，当我真正和他们深入交谈时，有点喝了几杯酒之后，他们会说，“是的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: One theory is there really aren't any secrets among the big labs.
zh: 一种理论是，在大实验室之间真的没有任何秘密。

speaker: Unknown
en: The big labs kind of all have the same information and they kind of have all the same knowledge and they lap each other on a regular basis, but there's not a lot of proprietary anything at this point.
zh: 大实验室基本上都有相同的信息，他们基本上都有相同的知识，他们定期互相超越，但在目前并没有多少专有的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then again, evidence of that is DeepSeek came out of left field and basically was like a re-implementation of a lot of the ideas under American big labs and had some original ideas of its own.
zh: 还有，这方面的证据是 DeepSeek 横空出世，基本上是对美国大实验室下许多想法的重新实现，并且有一些自己的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: But, wow, it wasn't that hard for some basically a hedge fund in China to do it, and so how much defensibility is there.
zh: 但是，哇，对于中国的一个基本上是对冲基金的机构来说，做到这一点并不那么难，那么有多大的可防御性呢。

speaker: Unknown
en: But on the other side of it, you've got, wow, these big labs are now paying individual engineers like they're rock stars and they're incredibly bright and creative people.
zh: 但在另一方面，你有，哇，这些大实验室现在像对待摇滚明星一样对待个别工程师，他们非常聪明和有创造力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe there's a dozen nascent ideas at any one of these labs that is actually going to be a huge breakthrough that's going to be hard to replicate.
zh: 也许这些实验室中的任何一个在任何一个时刻都有十几个初创的想法，实际上会成为一个巨大的突破，将很难复制。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so again, it's just like, I think we just need... I don't know, my view is I need to put a big discount on my forecasting ability on this one.
zh: 还有又一次，就像，我想我们只是需要……我不知道，我的观点是我需要对我的预测能力打一个很大的折扣。

speaker: Unknown
en: For me, it's much less interesting to try to say, "Okay, as a consequence, industry structure in five years is going to be X, the big winner and the category is going to be company Y, the big product killer app is going to be Z."
zh: 对我来说，试图说，“好吧，因此，五年后的行业结构将是 X，该类别的大赢家将是公司 Y，大型产品杀手级应用将是 Z，”并没有那么有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, I don't think I can predict that.
zh: 就像，我不认为我能预测那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think a much better use of my time is being very flexible and adaptable at a time like this.
zh: 我认为更好地利用我的时间是在这样一个时期非常灵活和适应。

speaker: Unknown
en: So with all this in mind, do you feel like there's something you're paying attention to more to help you decide, okay, this is where we want to place our bet, or is the answer essentially the strategy you guys have, which is place a lot of bets?
zh: 所以考虑到所有这些，你觉得有什么是你更关注的，以帮助你决定，好吧，这就是我们要下注的地方，还是答案基本上就是你们 guys 的策略，也就是下很多注？

speaker: Unknown
en: You guys raised the largest fund in history.
zh: 你们 guys 募集了历史上最大的基金。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the way you win in this world?
zh: 这就是你在这个世界里获胜的方式吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, for us, yeah.
zh: 我的意思是，对我们来说，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: For us, we obviously have a very deliberate strategy.
zh: 对我们来说，我们显然有一个非常深思熟虑的策略。

speaker: Unknown
en: One way to think about this the Peter Thiel... You remember the Peter Thiel formulation of... he said, "There's a two by two, there's optimism and pessimism, and then there's determinant and, is it indeterminate, and indeterminate."
zh: 思考这个问题的一种方式是 Peter Thiel……你记得 Peter Thiel 的……公式吗，他说，“有一个二乘二矩阵，有乐观主义和悲观主义，然后有决定论和，是不是不定论，和不定论。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so he always argued that Silicon Valley is characterized by too much what he calls indeterminate optimism.
zh: 他一直认为，硅谷的特征是他所谓的太多的不定论的乐观主义。

speaker: Unknown
en: What he meant by that is basically, I think the way he would describe it is an indeterminate optimist who thinks the world is going to be better but can't explain why.
zh: 他的意思基本上是，我想他会描述为一种不确定的乐观主义者，认为世界会变好但无法解释原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some combination of things is going to happen to make the world be better even if we don't know what those things are.
zh: 某些事情的结合将会发生，让世界变得更好，即使我们不知道那些事情是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think he at least historically would say that's basically... that risks at least being just wishful thinking or delusional thinking.
zh: 我认为他至少在历史上会说这基本上是……这至少有成为一厢情愿的想法或妄想的风险。

speaker: Unknown
en: What the world needs more is determinant optimists, which are people who are like, "No, the world is going to be better because I'm going to do this specific thing."
zh: 世界更需要的是确定的乐观主义者，他们是那种会说，“不，世界会变好，因为我要做这件具体的事情”的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: He would classify, for example, Elon, he would sort of maybe say VCs are indeterminate optimists and then he would say Elon is the determinant optimist where it's like, no, I'm going to build the electric car, I'm going to do solar, and then I'm going to do Mars and these very concrete things.
zh: 他会把……例如，Elon 分类，他可能会说风险投资人是不确定的乐观主义者，然后他会说 Elon 是确定的乐观主义者，就像那样，不，我要造电动汽车，我要做太阳能，然后我要做火星和这些非常具体的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's a lot to Peter's framework, but the way I would describe it is I think maybe if he and I disagree with part of that it would be I think the indeterminate optimism is a stronger phenomenon than at least I think he's historically represented it as, and I would put myself firmly in the indeterminate optimist category, and that's the strategy that we have at a16z which is... and the reason for that is hopefully it's not so much wishful thinking.
zh: 我认为 Peter 的框架有很多可取之处，但我描述它的方式是，我想也许如果他和我在那部分上有分歧，那就是我认为不确定的乐观主义是一种比至少我以前认为他所代表的现象更强的现象，我会坚定地把自己归入不确定的乐观主义者类别，这就是我们在 a16z 的策略，也就是……原因是希望这不太像是一厢情愿的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's more, no, the indeterminate optimism of venture capital or the indeterminate optimism of a16z or Silicon Valley is actually very specific which is there are these extremely bright and capable people, like Elon and many others, who are founders and kind of product creators.
zh: 更多的是，不，风险投资的不确定乐观主义或 a16z 或硅谷的不确定乐观主义实际上是非常具体的，因为有这些极其聪明和有能力的人，像 Elon 和许多其他人，他们是创始人，也是产品创造者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Each of those individual people is a determinant optimist.
zh: 这些个人中的每一个都是确定的乐观主义者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Each of them individually has a very strong view of what they're going to do, but the great virtue of the capitalist system, the great virtue of the American economy, the great virtue of Silicon Valley is we don't just have one of those and we don't just have 10 of those.
zh: 他们每个人都有一个非常强烈的关于他们要做什么的观点，但资本主义制度的巨大优点，美国经济的巨大优点，硅谷的巨大优点是我们不仅仅只有那些人中的一个，我们不仅仅只有那些人中的 10 个。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have 100 and a thousand and then 10,000 of those.
zh: 我们有 100 个、1000 个，然后是 10000 个那样的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: The way to optimize the outcome is to have as many of those as possible be as good as possible, run as hard as possible.
zh: 优化结果的方法是让尽可能多这样的人变得尽可能好，尽可能努力地奔跑。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then just the nature of the future is like we just don't know all the answers and that's okay.
zh: 然后未来的本质就是这样，我们只是不知道所有的答案，但这没关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the right way to deal with that is to run as many experiments as possible and have as many smart people try to do as many interesting things as possible.
zh: 然后应对这一点的正确方法是进行尽可能多的实验，并让尽可能多的聪明人尝试做尽可能多有趣的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, yeah, I would put myself firmly on the side of the indeterminate optimistic.
zh: 所以，是的，我会坚定地站在不确定乐观主义的一边。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm wondering if the answer to the question of what you look for now more and more is this determinant optimistic founder that has this massive ambition and is-
zh: 我想知道，对于你现在越来越多地寻找什么这个问题的答案，是否就是这种确定的乐观主义创始人，他拥有这种巨大的雄心并且是……

speaker: Unknown
en: ... [inaudible 01:21:00] Optimistic founder-
zh: …… [听不清 01:21:00] 乐观主义创始人……

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... and has this massive ambition and is actually working on achieving it.
zh: ……并且拥有这种巨大的雄心并且正在努力实现它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, yeah.
zh: 是的，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: No, that's right, that's right.
zh: 不，没错，没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean look, the founders need to be determinate optimists.
zh: 我的意思是看，创始人必须是确定的乐观主义者。

speaker: Unknown
en: They need to have a very specific plan.
zh: 他们必须有一个非常具体的计划。

speaker: Unknown
en: And look, the critique always... The critique from the founders is, "Oh UVCs have it easy, because you don't actually have to commit, right?
zh: 而且，看，批评总是……来自创始人的批评是，“噢，UVC 日子好过，因为你们实际上不必承诺，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't actually have to, like, make... You have to make the bed you lay in, you can, like, place multiple bets you can have.
zh: 你们实际上不必，像，做……你们必须铺好你们睡的床，你可以，像，下多个注，你可以有。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whereas a portfolio, you should have a lot more sympathy for us as founders, because we only get to make the one bet."
zh: 而对于投资组合，你应该对我们作为创始人给予更多的同情，因为我们只能下那个注。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's truth to that.
zh: 那是有道理的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The kind of argument on that is the founders get to run their companies, we don't.
zh: 关于那点的论证是创始人可以经营他们的公司，而我们不行。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we don't get to put our hand on the steering wheel.
zh: 所以，我们不能把手放在方向盘上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, the great virtue of being a determinant optimist is you actually get to single-mindedly execute against that goal.
zh: 所以，成为确定的乐观主义者的巨大优点是，你实际上可以针对那个目标进行专注的执行。

speaker: Unknown
en: And look, in the long run, who does history remember?
zh: 而且，看，从长远来看，历史记住的是谁？

speaker: Unknown
en: History remembers Henry Ford, right, not whoever was, whatever the seed investor who seeded Ford Motor Company, and 10 other car companies have failed.
zh: 历史记住的是 Henry Ford，对吧，而不是那个种下了福特汽车公司种子的种子投资人，以及另外 10 家失败的汽车公司的任何人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, the determinant optimist is the founder of the company builder and the engineer, and these are the people who actually use the sign, and deserve 99.9999% of the credit.
zh: 所以，确定的乐观主义者是公司的创始人、建设者和工程师，这些人实际上是真正使用符号的人，并且值得获得 99.9999% 的功劳。

speaker: Unknown
en: But you know, having said that, I do think there is a role for having some indeterminate optimists in the background, no, helping along the way, and helping keep the whole cycle going.
zh: 但是你知道，话虽如此，我确实认为在背景中有一些不确定的乐观主义者的角色是有用的，不，在一路提供帮助，并帮助保持整个循环继续。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think about AGI in shifting your investment thesis?
zh: 你会考虑 AGI 来改变你的投资论点吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Like, as we approach AGI and hit AGI, as an investor, how do you think about your investment thesis changing?
zh: 就像，随着我们接近 AGI 并实现 AGI，作为一个投资者，你如何看待你的投资论点的变化？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, so I've always kind of had a little bit of an issue... I've always kind of struggled with the concept of AGI because at least... Well, let's put it this way.
zh: 是的，所以我一直有点问题……我一直在为 AGI 的概念而挣扎，因为至少……好吧，这样说吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's define terms which is where I kind of struggle with it.
zh: 让我们定义术语，这就是我对此有点纠结的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Which is, there's like the prosaic definition of AGI, and then there's like the cosmic definition.
zh: 也就是，有 AGI 的平淡定义，然后有宇宙定义。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the way I describe it as, so let me start with the cosmic one.
zh: 我描述它的方式是，所以让我从宇宙定义开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the cosmic one is basically, is the singularity, right?
zh: 所以宇宙定义基本上是奇点，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, AGI is the moment where you enter the singularity which is to say where the world fundamentally changes.
zh: 所以，AGI 是你进入奇点的时刻，也就是说世界发生了根本性的变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the rules of the old world are gone, we're now operating in a new domain.
zh: 旧世界的规则消失了，我们现在在一个新的领域运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the full definition of singularity is it's a world in which human judgment is no longer really relevant because you get this self-improvement loop, the AI is improving itself.
zh: 奇点的完整定义是一个人类判断不再真正相关的世界，因为你得到了这个自我改进循环，AI 在改进自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: In a sort of race circle takeoff scenarios, you could see if this takeoff thing, where the AI's improving itself, and the machines are making decisions so much faster than people, and people are just sitting there watching the machine do its thing.
zh: 在一种竞赛循环起飞的场景中，你可以看到如果这个起飞的事情，AI 在改进自己，机器做决策的速度比人快得多，人们只是坐在那里看着机器做它的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I kind of described it, I don't really think we live in that world, whether they could call that utopian or dystopian, I don't think we're lucky or unlucky enough to live in that world.
zh: 我有点描述它，我真的不认为我们生活在那个世界里，无论他们称之为乌托邦还是反乌托邦，我不认为我们足够幸运或不幸生活在那个世界里。

speaker: Unknown
en: We could debate that, we could talk about that more.
zh: 我们可以辩论这一点，我们可以更多地谈论它。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the prosaic definition of AGI that at least I think the industry purchases but it's kind of conversed on, and tell me if you agree with this, is when the AI could do every economically-relevant task as good as a person.
zh: 但是 AGI 的平淡定义，至少我认为行业购买但有点被讨论的，告诉我你是否同意这一点，是当 AI 可以像人一样好地完成每一项经济相关任务时。

speaker: Unknown
en: The way the co-founder of Anthropic put it is, like, "A basket of the most valuable economic tasks," so it's, like, 10, 15, not every single economically-valuable task.
zh: Anthropic 的联合创始人描述它的方式是，就像，“一篮子最有价值的任务”，所以它就像，10，15，不是每一项单独的经济有价值的任务。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, got it.
zh: 好的，明白了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, so it's maybe even a slightly reduced definition.
zh: 是的，所以这可能甚至是一个稍微缩减的定义。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, we're clearly getting close to that if we're not already there.
zh: 顺便说一句，如果我们还没达到那一点的话，我们显然已经接近了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so on that one, I kind of feel like, so I kind of feel like the cosmic one overstates what's going to happen.
zh: 所以关于那一点，我有点觉得，我有点觉得宇宙式的那个说法夸大了将要发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I kind of feel like the kind of AGI definition that you just gave, I think it kind of understates what's going to happen.
zh: 然后我有点觉得你刚才给出的那种 AGI 定义，我觉得它有点低估了将要发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's almost too reductionist.
zh: 这几乎太简化了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason for that is, I don't think there's any reason to assume that human skill level is the cap on anything.
zh: 原因是，我认为没有任何理由假设人类技能水平是任何事物的上限。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the way we say that is AGI always is the definition you gave, the definition I gave.
zh: 所以我们要表达的是，AGI 总是你给出的定义，我给出的定义。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's always kind of relative in comparison to a human worker, right?
zh: 它总是相对于人类工作者进行比较的，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's, like, I don't know, human skill level caps out at a certain point, but that's because of the inherent biological limitations of the human organism.
zh: 而且，就像，我不知道，人类技能水平在某个点会封顶，但那是由于人类机体的固有生物学限制。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Human, I gave you an example.
zh: 人类，我给你举了个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Human IQ, kind of what they call "fluid intelligence," or the sort of G factor of fluid intelligence, IQ I think tops out in humans as a species, it tops out around 160.
zh: 人类智商，也就是他们所谓的「流体智力」，或者流体智力的 G 因素，我认为作为一个物种，智商在人类身上封顶，封顶在 160 左右。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where at like 160 it's like Einstein level, Einstein [inaudible 01:25:00]-
zh: 在 160 的时候就像爱因斯坦级别，爱因斯坦 [听不清 01:25:00]-

speaker: Unknown
en: In terms of IQ.
zh: 就智商而言。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... in terms of IQ.
zh: ……就智商而言。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like, it just tops out at 160.
zh: 就像，它只是在 160 封顶。

speaker: Unknown
en: The 160 IQ people are the ones who come up with new physics, there's only a small handful of those.
zh: 那些 160 智商的人是提出新物理学的人，只有一小撮这样的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Generally speaking, when we run into somebody in the world who's like incredibly smart, who's like a bestselling author, or like a, you know, one of the world's best, I don't know, research scientists, or one of the world's best doctors, whatever it would be, probably 140 is kind of the IQ that you're looking for there.
zh: 一般来说，当我们在世界上遇到一个极其聪明的人，比如畅销书作家，或者，你知道，世界上最好的，我不知道，研究科学家之一，或者世界上最好的医生之一，不管是什么，大概是 140 是你在那里寻找的智商。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're looking for a really good lawyer, it's probably 130.
zh: 如果你找一个真正好的律师，大概是 130。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're looking for a really good line manager in a business, it's probably 110.
zh: 如果你在企业里找一个真正好的线经理，大概是 110。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're looking for an accountant, like a small business accountant, who's good at doing the books for small businesses, it's probably 105.
zh: 如果你找一个会计师，比如一个小企业会计师，擅长为小企业做账的，大概是 105。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the kind of scope of impressive human... The ability of the human organism to do intellectually impressive things, it's sort of that 110 to 160 is kind of the spectrum, and good news is there's a lot of those people running around, but there's not that many at 140, 150, 160.
zh: 所以那种令人印象深刻的人类能力的范围……人类机体在智力上做令人印象深刻的事情的能力，大概就是那个 110 到 160 的范围，好消息是到处都有很多这样的人，但在 140、150、160 的并没有那么多。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's like, that's like the limitations of what can fit in here, right?
zh: 但就像，那是这里能容纳的局限，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like, there's no theoretical limit on where this goes if you release the limitations of human biology, right?
zh: 而且，就像，如果你解除人类生物学的限制，这对发展没有理论上的限制，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, can you have a... And you already have people running these experiments to kind of do human-equivalent kind of IQ for existing AI models.

And any of you guys, there's a small handful of you guys who have access to these people, you could have the world's leading experts in the domain actually show up.

en: Have you not heard of it?

en: Okay, so you're going to really enjoy it.

en: So, I won't spoil too much of it, so at the surface level the following spoils nothing.

speaker: Unknown

zh: 有一个由 Joaquin Phoenix 饰演的警长，他就像一个固执的、基本属于右翼的人，还有一个由 Pedro Pascal 饰演的市长，他基本上是一个年轻的、时髦的、进步人士。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the movie starts I think in March of 2020.

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it starts when COVID first hits.
zh: 所以它从 COVID 刚爆发时开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it sort of as it plays out over the next few months, it intersects, and it sort of extends into the summer of 2020.
zh: 然后随着接下来几个月的发展，各种事件交织在一起，它一直延续到 2020 年夏天。

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there's a third kind of element to it which is there's a company which is basically a loosely-disguised version of Meta if you read the backstory of it, which is building an AI data center on the outskirts of town.
zh: 然后还有第三个元素，有一家公司，基本上是 Meta 的伪装版，如果你阅读它的背景故事的话，它正在镇郊建立一个人工智能数据中心。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they kind of pull that in as sort of a thing that looms larger and larger over time.
zh: 所以他们把这个作为一个随时间推移越来越重要的因素引入。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the thing it really is great at is it really shows, you know, this is a small town in New Mexico.
zh: 这部电影真正伟大的地方在于它展示了，你知道，这是新墨西哥州的一个小镇。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, everybody in the town gets full wrapped up in all the COVID stuff, and they get fully wrapped up in all the BLM stuff, and they get fully wrapped up in all the tech anxiety stuff.
zh: 所以镇上的每个人都完全卷入了所有 COVID 的很多事情，他们完全卷入了所有 BLM 的很多事情，他们完全卷入了所有关于科技焦虑的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But they're all experiencing it basically through the internet, right?

speaker: Unknown
en: Which is what actually happened, right?

speaker: Unknown
en: So the reason I love the movie so much is one is it's the first movie that directly grapples with 2020, of what happened in 2020, and it just, like, fully, fully engages and grapples with all the dynamics that were playing out in the country.
zh: 所以我如此喜爱这部电影的原因之一是，它是第一部直接直面 2020 年、直面 2020 年发生的事情的电影，它只是，就像，完全地、完全地投入并应对当时在全国上演的所有动态。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the other reason is it's the first movie that does a really good job of showing what it was like especially in that area to live in a world in which there were things happening in the real world, and people were kind of experiencing events online, like in a way that was very central in their lives.
zh: 但另一个原因是，它是第一部做得非常好的电影，展示了在那个地区生活在这样一个世界是什么样子的，现实世界中正在发生事情，而人们基本上是在网上体验事件，就像这种方式在他们生活中非常核心。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I wouldn't even say I completely agree with the movie or whatever, and I think the director of the movie and I would probably disagree about a lot, but he really tries hard to really grapple with what it's actually like to live like a human being in the 2020s in America in a way that I think many other filmmakers who are very talented have just been very scared of touching.
zh: 所以我甚至不会说我完全同意这部电影或别的什么，而且我认为这部电影导演和我可能会在很多事情上意见分歧，但他非常努力地真正试图去应对作为一个人类在 2020 年代的美国生活实际上是什么样子的，而我认为许多其他非常有才华的电影制作人一直非常害怕触及这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this guy, for some reason he's just like, "Yeah, I'm just going find all the third rails and I'm just going to fucking grab them."
zh: 而这家伙，出于某种原因，他就像，“是的，我就要去找所有的禁忌话题，我就要他妈地去抓住它们。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I can see why that's your favorite movie of the year.

speaker: Unknown
en: It's great, it's great, it's great.

en: Oh man.
